text.DRB: And he remembered the malice of the children of Bean, which were to the people a snare and a scandal, lying in wait for them in the way.
text.KJV: Also he remembred the iniurie of the children of Bean, who had bene a snare and an offence vnto the people, in that they lay in waite for them in the wayes.

text.DRB: And they were shut up by him in towers, and he came near to them, and anathematized them, and burnt their towers with fire, with all that were in them.
text.KJV: Hee shut them vp therefore in the towres, and incamped against them, and destroyed them vtterly, and burnt the towers of that place with fire, and all that were therein.

text.DRB: And he passed to the children of Ammon, and found a strong band, and a copious people, and Timothee their captain:
text.KJV: Afterward he passed ouer to the children of Ammon, where he found a mighty power, and much people, with Timotheus their captaine.

text.DRB: and he made many battles with them, and they were discomfited in their sight, & he struck them:
text.KJV: So he fought many battels with them, till at length they were discomfited before him; and he smote them.

text.DRB: and he took Gazer the city, and her daughters, and returned into Jewry.
text.KJV: And when hee had taken Iazar, with the townes belonging thereto, he returned into Iudea.

text.DRB: And the Gentiles that are in Galaad, gathered together against the Israelites, that were in their coasts to dispatch them: and they fled into the fortress of Datheman.
text.KJV: Then the heathen that were at Galead, assembled themselues together against the Israelites that were in their quarters to destroy them: but they fled to the fortresse of Dathema;

text.DRB: And they sent letters to Judas, and his brethren, saying: The heathen round about are gathered together against us, to dispatch us:
text.KJV: And sent letters vnto Iudas and his brethren: The heathen that are round about vs, are assembled together against vs to destroy vs;

text.DRB: and they prepare to come, and to occupy the fortress into which we are fled: and Timothee is the captain of their host.
text.KJV: And they are preparing to come and take the fortresse whereunto wee are fled, Timotheus being captaine of their host.

text.DRB: Now therefore come, & deliver us, out of their hands, because a multitude of us is fallen.
text.KJV: Come now therefore and deliuer vs from their handes, for many of vs are slaine.

text.DRB: And all our brethren, that were in the places of Tubin, are slain: and they led away their wives captives, and the children, and the spoils, and they have slain there almost a thousand men.
text.KJV: Yea all our brethren that were in the places of Tobie, are put to death, their wiues and their children; Also they haue caried away captiues, and borne away their stuffe, and they haue destroied there about a thousand men.

text.DRB: And the epistles were yet in reading, and lo other messengers came out of Galilee their coats rent, reporting according to these words:
text.KJV: While these letters were yet reading, behold there came other messengers from Galilee with their clothes rent, who reported on this wise,

text.DRB: saying that there were assembled against them from Ptolemais, and Tyre, and Sidon, and all Galilee is replenished with aliens, to consume us.
text.KJV: And said: They of Ptolemais, and of Tyrus, and Sidon, and all Galilee of the Gentiles are assembled together against vs to consume vs.

text.DRB: And as Judas and the people heard these words, a great assembly assembled together to consider what they should do for their brethren, that were in tribulation, and were expugned of them.
text.KJV: Now when Iudas and the people heard these wordes, there assembled a great congregation together, to consult what they should doe for their brethren, that were in trouble and assaulted of them.
Then said Iudas vnto Simon his brother, Choose thee out men, and goe, and deliuer thy brethren that are in Galilee, for I and Ionathan my brother, will goe into the countrey of Galaad.
text.NABRE: 5:17 Judas said to his brother Simon: “Choose men for yourself, and go, rescue your kindred in Galilee; my brother Jonathan and I will go to Gilead.”
text.NRSV: Judas said to his brother Simon, “Choose your men and go and rescue your kindred in Galilee; Jonathan my brother and I will go to Gilead.”
text.DRB: And Judas said to Simon his brother: Choose thee men, and go, and deliver thy brethren in Galilee: and I, and my brother Jonathas will go into the country of Galaad.
And he left Joseph the son of Zacharie, and Azarias, captains of the people with the residue of the army in Jurie to keep it:
text.KJV: So hee left Ioseph the sonne of Zacharias, and Azarias captaines of the people, with the remnant of the hoste in Iudea to keepe it,

text.DRB: and he commanded them, saying: Be ye over this people: & make no battle against the heathen, till we return.
text.KJV: Unto whom he gaue commandement, saying, Take yee the charge of this people, and see that you make not warre against the heathen, vntill the time that we come againe.

text.DRB: And there were parted to Simon three thousand men, to go into Galilee: but to Judas eight thousand to go into the country of Galaad.
text.KJV: Now vnto Simon were giuen three thousand men to goe into Galilee, and vnto Iudas eight thousand men for the countrey of Galaad.

text.DRB: And Simon went into Galilee, & made many battles with the heathen: and the heathen were discomfited before his face, and he pursued them even to the gate of Ptolemais.
text.KJV: Then went Simon into Galilee, where hee fought many battels with the heathen, so that the heathen were discomfited by him.

text.DRB: And there fell of the heathen almost three thousand men, & he took the spoils of them,
text.KJV: And hee pursued them vnto the gate of Ptolemais; And there were slaine of the heathen about three thousand men, whose spoiles he tooke.

text.DRB: and he took them that were in Galilee & in Arbatis with their wives, and children, & all things that they had, and he brought them into Jurie with great joy.
text.KJV: And those that were in Galilee and in Arbattis, with their wiues and their children, and all that they had, tooke he away and brought them into Iudea, with great ioy.

text.DRB: And Judas Machabeus, and Jonathas his brother passed over Jordan, and went three days' journey through the desert.
text.KJV: Iudas Maccabeus also and his brother Ionathan, went ouer Iordan, and trauailed three dayes iourney in the wildernesse,

text.DRB: And the Nabutheians met them, & received them peaceably, & told them all things, that had happened to their brethren in the country of Galaad,
text.KJV: Where they met with the Nabathites, who came vnto them in peaceable maner, and told them euery thing that had happened to their brethren in the land of Galaad,

text.DRB: and that many of them were comprehended in Barasa, and Bosor, and in Alimis, and in Casphor, & Mageth, & Carnaim, all these cities fenced, & great.
text.KJV: And how that many of them were shut vp in Bosora, and Bosor, in Alema, Casphor, Maked & Carnaim (all these cities are strong and great.)

text.DRB: Yea and in the rest of the cities of Galaad they are held captive, & on the morrow they appointed to remove the army near to these cities, and to take them, & to dispatch them in one day.
text.KJV: And that they were shut vp in the rest of the cities of the countrey of Galaad, and that against to morrow they had appointed to bring their host against the forts, and to take them, and to destroy them all in one day.

text.DRB: And Judas turned & his army the way into Bosor suddenly, and took the city: and he slew every male in the edge of the sword, & took all their spoils, & burnt it with fire.
text.KJV: Hereupon Iudas and his host turned suddenly by the way of the wildernesse vnto Bosorra, and when he had wonne the citie, hee slew all the males with the edge of the sword, and tooke all their spoiles, and burnt the citie with fire.

text.DRB: And they arose thence by night, & went even to the fortress.
text.KJV: From whence hee remooued by night, and went till he came to the fortresse.

text.DRB: And it came to pass early in the morning, when they had lifted up their eyes, & behold much people, whereof there was no number, carrying ladders, & engines to take the fortress, and they expugned them.
text.KJV: And betimes in the morning they looked vp, & behold, there was an innumerable people bearing ladders, and other engines of warre, to take the fortresse: for they assaulted them.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that the battle began, and the cry of the battle ascended to heaven as a trumpet, and a great cry out of the city:
text.KJV: When Iudas therefore saw that the battaile was begun, and that the cry of the citie went vp to heauen, with trumpets, and a great sound,

text.DRB: & he said to his host: Fight ye today for your brethren.
text.KJV: He said vnto his hoste, Fight this day for your brethren.

text.DRB: And he came with three ranks behind them & they cried out with trumpets, & cried in prayer.
text.KJV: So he went foorth behinde them in three companies, who sounded their trumpets, and cryed with prayer.

text.DRB: And the camp of Timothee understood that it was Machabeus, and they fled back from his presence: and they struck them with a great plague: and there fell of them in that day almost eight thousand men.
text.KJV: Then the hoste of Timotheus knowing that it was Maccabeus, fled from him: wherefore hee smote them with a great slaughter: so that there were killed of them that day about eight thousand men.

text.DRB: And Judas turned aside into Maspha, and expugned, & took it, and he slew every male thereof, & he took the spoils thereof, & burnt it with fire.
text.KJV: This done, Iudas turned aside to Maspha, and after he had assaulted it, hee tooke it, and slewe all the males therein, and receiued the spoiles therof, and burnt it with fire.

text.DRB: From thence he marched, and took Casbon, and Mageth, and Bosor, & the rest of the cities of Galaad.
text.KJV: From thence went he, and tooke Casphon, Maged, Bosor, and the other cities of the countrey of Galaad.

text.DRB: But after these words Timothee gathered another army, and camped against Raphon beyond the torrent.
text.KJV: After these things, gathered Timotheus another hoste, and encamped against Raphon beyond the brooke.

text.DRB: And Judas send to view the army: and they reported unto him, saying: That all nations, that are round about us, are assembled against us, an army exceeding great:
text.KJV: So Iudas sent to espie the hoste, who brought him word, saying; All the heathen that be round about vs, are assembled vnto them, euen a very great hoste.

text.DRB: and they have hired the Arabians to help them, & they have camped beyond the torrent, being ready to come unto thee into battle. And Judas went to meet them.
text.KJV: Hee hath also hired the Arabians to helpe them, and they haue pitched their tents beyond the brooke, readie to come and fight against thee: vpon this Iudas went to meet them.

text.DRB: And Timothee said to the princes of his army: When Judas shall approach, & his army to the torrent of water: if he pass over unto us first, we shall not be able to sustain him: because prevailing he will prevail against us.
text.KJV: Then Timotheus said vnto the captaines of his hoste, When Iudas and his hoste come neere the brooke, if he passe ouer first vnto vs, we shall not be able to withstand him, for hee will mightily preuaile against vs.

text.DRB: But if he be afraid to pass over, and camp without the river, let us pass over to them, and we shall prevail against them.
text.KJV: But if he be afraid, and campe beyond the riuer, we shall goe ouer vnto him, and preuaile against him.

text.DRB: But as Judas approached to the torrent of water, he set the scribes of the people by the torrent, & commanded them, saying: Leave not a man: but let all come into the battle.
text.KJV: Now when Iudas came neere the brooke, he caused the Scribes of the people to remaine by the brooke: vnto whom hee gaue commandement, saying, Suffer no man to remaine in the campe, but let all come to the battell.

text.DRB: And he passed over to them first, & all the people after him, & all the heathen were discomfited at their presence, & they threw away their weapons, & fled to the temple, that was in Carnaim.
text.KJV: So he went first ouer vnto them, and all the people after him: then all the heathen being discomfited before him, cast away their weapons, and fled vnto the Temple that was at Carnaim.

text.DRB: And he took the city it-self, & the temple he burnt with fire, with all things that were therein: & Carnaim was subdued, & could not stand against the face of Judas.
text.KJV: But they tooke the citie, and burnt the Temple, with all that were therein. Thus was Carnaim subdued, neither could they stand any longer before Iudas.

text.DRB: And Judas gathered together all the Israelites, that were in the country of Galaad, from the least even to the greatest, and their wives, and children, and an army exceeding great, to come into the land of Juda.
text.KJV: Then Iudas gathered together all the Israelites that were in the countrey of Galaad from the least vnto the greatest, euen their wiues and their children, and their stuffe, a very great hoste, to the ende they might come into the land of Iudea.

text.DRB: And they came as far as Ephron: and this city was great, situate in the entrance, fenced exceedingly, and there was no means to decline from it on the right hand or on the left, but the way was through the midst.
text.KJV: Now when they came vnto Ephron (this was a great city in the way as they should goe, very well fortified) they could not turne from it, either on the right hand or the left, but must needs passe through the midst of it.

text.DRB: And they that were in the city, shut in themselves, and stopped the gates with stones: & Judas sent to them in peaceable words,
text.KJV: Then they of the city shut them out, and stopped vp the gates with stones.

text.DRB: saying: Let us pass through your land, and go into our country: and no man shall hurt you, only on foot we will pass. And they would not open unto them.
text.KJV: Whereupon Iudas sent vnto them in peaceable maner, saying; Let vs passe through your land to goe into our owne countrey, and none shall doe you any hurt, we will onely passe thorow on foote: howbeit they would not open vnto him.

text.DRB: And Judas commanded proclamation to be made in the camp, that they should approach every man in the place, wherein he was.
text.KJV: Wherefore Iudas commaunded a proclamation to be made throughout the hoste, that euery man should pitch his tent in the place where he was.

text.DRB: and the men of strength approached, and he assaulted that city all the day, and all the night, and the city was delivered into his hand:
text.KJV: So the souldiers pitched, and assaulted the city all that day, and all that night, till at the length the city was deliuered into his hands:

text.DRB: & they slew every male in the edge of the sword, and he rooted it up, and took the spoils thereof, & passed through all the city upon the slain.
text.KJV: Who then slew all the males with the edge of the sword, and rased the city, and tooke the spoiles therof, and passed through the city ouer them that were slaine.

text.DRB: And they passed over Jordan in the great plain, against the face of Bethsan.
text.KJV: After this went they ouer Iordan, into the great plaine before Bethsan.

text.DRB: And Judas gathered together the hindmost, & he exhorted the people throughout all the way, till they came into the land of Juda:
text.KJV: And Iudas gathered together those that came behind, and exhorted the people all the way through, till they came into the land of Iudea.

text.DRB: and they went up into mount Sion with joy and gladness, and offered holocausts because none of them was fallen till they returned in peace.
text.KJV: So they went vp to mount Sion with ioy and gladnesse, where they offered burnt offerings, because not one of them were slaine, vntill they had returned in peace.

text.DRB: And in the days that Judas & Jonathas were in the land of Galaad, & Simon his brother in Galilee against the face of Ptolomais,
text.KJV: Now what time as Iudas and Ionathan were in the land of Galaad, and Simon his brother in Galilee before Ptolemais,

text.DRB: Joseph the son of Zacharie heard, & Azarias, the princes of the band, the things well achieved, and the battles that were made,
text.KJV: Ioseph the sonne of Zacharias, and Azarias, captaines of the garisons, heard of the valiant actes and warlike deeds which they had done.

text.DRB: and he said: Let us also make us a name, and let us go fight against the heathen, that are round about us.
text.KJV: Wherefore they said, Let vs also get vs a name, and goe fight against the heathen that are round about vs.

text.DRB: And he commanded them that were in his army, and they went to Jamnia.
text.KJV: So when they had giuen charge vnto the garison that was with them, they went towards Iamnia.

text.DRB: And Gorgias went forth out of the city, and his men to meet them into battle.
text.KJV: Then came Gorgias and his men out of the citie to fight against them.

text.DRB: And Joseph and Azarias were chased in flight even unto the borders of Jurie: and there fell that day of the people of Israel about two thousand men, and there was made a great tumult in the people:
text.KJV: And so it was, that Ioseph and Azarias were put to flight, and pursued vnto the borders of Iudea, and there were slaine that day of the people of Israel about two thousand men.

text.DRB: because they heard not Judas, & his brethren, thinking that they should do manfully.
text.KJV: Thus was there a great ouerthrow among the children of Israel, because they were not obedient vnto Iudas, and his brethren, but thought to doe some valiant act.

text.DRB: But they were not of the seed of those men by whom salvation was made in Israel.
text.KJV: Moreouer these men came not of the seed of those, by whose hand deliuerance was giuen vnto Israel.

text.DRB: And the men of Juda were magnified exceedingly in the sight of all Israel, and of all nations where their name was heard.
text.KJV: Howbeit the man Iudas and his brethren were greatly renowned in the sight of all Israel, and of all the heathen wheresoeuer their name was heard of,

text.DRB: And they came together unto them, with acclamations for their good success.
text.KJV: Insomuch as the people assembled vnto them with ioyfull acclamations.

text.DRB: And Judas went out, and his brethren, and they expugned the children of Esau in the land, that is toward the South, and they struck Chebron, and her daughters: and the walls thereof and the towers he burnt with fire round about.
text.KJV: Afterward went Iudas foorth with his brethren, and fought against the children of Esau in the land toward the South, where he smote Hebron, and the townes thereof, and pulled downe the fortresse of it, and burnt the townes thereof round about.

text.DRB: And he removed the camp to go into the land of the aliens, and he walked through Samaria.
text.KJV: From thence he remoued to goe into the land of the Philistines, and passed through Samaria.

text.DRB: In that day fell the Priests in battle, whilst they will do manfully, whilst they go forth without counsel into battle.
text.KJV: At that time certaine priests desirous to shew their valour, were slaine in battell, for that they went out to fight vnaduisedly.

So Iudas turned to Azotus in the land of the Philistines, and when he had pulled downe their altars, and burnt their carued images with fire, and spoiled their cities, he returned into the land of Iudea.
text.NABRE: 5:68 Judas then turned toward Azotus in the land of the foreigners. He destroyed their altars and burned the carved images of their gods; and after plundering their cities he returned to the land of Judah.
text.NRSV: Then Judas turned aside to Azotus in the land of the Philistines. He tore down their altars, and the carved images of their gods he burned with fire. After he had plundered the towns, he returned to the land of Judah.
text.DRB: And Judas declined to Azotus into the land of the aliens, and he threw down their altars, and the sculptiles of their gods he burnt with fire: and he took the spoils of the cities, and returned into the land of Juda.

text.KJV: About that time king Antiochus trauailing through the high countreys, heard say that Elimais in the countrey of Persia, was a citie greatly renowned for riches, siluer, and gold,
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 As King Antiochus passed through the eastern provinces, he heard that in Persia there was a city, Elam, famous for its wealth in silver and gold,
text.NRSV: King Antiochus was going through the upper provinces when he heard that Elymais in Persia was a city famed for its wealth in silver and gold.
text.DRB: And king Antiochus walked through the higher countries, and he heard that there was a city Elymais in Persis, most noble, and plentiful in silver & gold,
and a temple therein exceeding rich: and coverings thereof gold, and brigantines, and shields, which Alexander Philip's son the king of Macedonia that reigned first in Greece left there.
text.KJV: And that there was in it a very rich temple, wherein were couerings of gold, and brestplates, and shields which Alexander sonne of Philippe the Macedonian King, who reigned first among the Grecians, had left there.

text.DRB: And he came, and sought to take the city, and to spoil it, and he could not, because the word was known to them that were in the city.
text.KJV: Wherefore he came and sought to take the citie, and to spoile it, but he was not able, because they of the citie hauing had warning thereof,

text.DRB: And they rose up into battle, and he fled from thence, and departed with great sorrow, and returned into Babylonia.
text.KJV: Rose vp against him in battell: So he fled and departed thence with great heauinesse, and returned to Babylon.

text.DRB: And there came one that told him in Persis, that the camps that were in the land of Juda, were put to flight:
text.KJV: Moreouer there came one, who brought in tidings into Persia, that the armies which went against the land of Iudea, were put to flight:

text.DRB: and that Lysias went with great power with the first, and was put to flight before the face of the Jews, and they grew strong with armour, and force, and many spoils: which they took out of the camps, which they destroyed:
text.KJV: And that Lysias who went forth first with a great power, was driuen away of the Iewes, and that they were made strong by the armour, and power, and store of spoiles, which they had gotten of the armies, whom they had destroyed.

text.DRB: and that they threw down the abomination which they had builded upon the altar that was in Jerusalem, and the sanctification they compassed about as before with high walls, yea and Bethsura his city,
text.KJV: Also that they had pulled downe the abomination which hee had set vp vpon the altar in Ierusalem, and that they had compassed about the Sanctuarie with high wals as before, and his citie Bethsura.
Now when the king heard these words, he was astonished, and sore moued, whereupon hee laide him downe vpon his bedde, and fell sicke for griefe, because it had not befallen him, as hee looked for.
text.NABRE: 6:8 When the king heard this news, he was astonished and very much shaken. Sick with grief because his designs had failed, he took to his bed.
text.NRSV: When the king heard this news, he was astounded and badly shaken. He took to his bed and became sick from grief, because things had not turned out for him as he had planned.
text.DRB: And it came to pass as the king heard these words, he was sore afraid, and was moved exceedingly: and he lay down upon his bed, and fell into sickness for sorrow, because it was not chanced to him as he thought.
And he was there for many days: because there was renewed in him great sorrowfulness, and he made account to die.
text.KJV: And there hee continued many dayes: for his griefe was euer more and more, and he made account that he should die.

text.DRB: And he called all his friends, & said to them: sleep is departed from mine eyes, and I am fallen away, and my heart is gone for pensiveness:
text.KJV: Wherefore he called for all his friends, and said vnto them, The sleepe is gone from mine eyes, and my heart faileth for very care.

text.DRB: and I said in my heart: Into how great tribulation am I come, & into what waves of sorrow, wherein now I am: who was pleasant, and beloved in my power!
text.KJV: And I thought with my selfe: Into what tribulation am I come, and how great a flood is it wherein now I am? for I was bountifull, and beloued in my power.

text.DRB: But now I remember the evils, that I have done in Jerusalem, from whence also I took away all the spoils of gold, and of silver, that were in it, and I sent to take away the inhabitants of Jewry, without cause.
text.KJV: But now I remember the euils that I did at Ierusalem, and that I tooke all the vessels of gold and siluer that were therein, and sent to destroy the inhabitants of Iudea without a cause.

text.DRB: I know therefore that for this cause have these evils found me: and lo I perish with great sorrow in a strange country.
text.KJV: I perceiue therefore that for this cause these troubles are come vpon me, and behold I perish through great griefe in a strange land.

text.DRB: And he called Philip, one of his friends, & he made him chief over all his kingdom.
text.KJV: Then called he for Philip one of his friends whom he made ruler ouer all his realme:

text.DRB: And he gave him the crown, and his robe, and ring, that he should bring Antiochus his son, and should bring him up to reign.
text.KJV: And gaue him the crowne and his robe, and his signet, to the end hee should bring vp his sonne Antiochus, and nourish him vp for the kingdome.
So king Antiochus died there in the hundreth forty and ninth yeere.
text.NABRE: 6:16 So King Antiochus died there in the one hundred and forty-ninth year.
text.NRSV: So King Antiochus died there in the one hundred forty-ninth year.
text.DRB: And Antiochus the king died there in the year an hundreth forty nine.

text.KJV: Now when Lysias knew that the king was dead, he set vp Antiochus his sonne (whom he had brought vp being yong) to reigne in his stead, and his name he called Eupator.
text.NABRE: 6:17 When Lysias learned that the king was dead, he set up the king’s son Antiochus, whom he had reared as a child, to be king in his place; and he gave him the title Eupator.
text.NRSV: When Lysias learned that the king was dead, he set up the king’s son Antiochus to reign in his place. He had reared him from boyhood, and he named him Eupator.
text.DRB: And Lysias understood that the king was dead, and he appointed Antiochus his son to reign, who he brought up a young man: and he called his name Eupator.
And they that were in the castle, had inclosed Israel round about the holy places: and they sought them evils always, and the strengthening of the Gentiles.
text.KJV: About this time they that were in the towre shut vp the Israelites round about the Sanctuarie, and sought alwayes their hurt, and the strengthening of the heathen.

text.DRB: And Judas thought to destroy them: and he called together all the people, to besiege them.
text.KJV: Wherefore Iudas purposing to destroy them, called all the people together to besiege them.

text.DRB: And they came together, and besieged them in the year an hundredth fifty, and they made balists and engines.
text.KJV: So they came together, and besieged them in the hundred and fiftith yeere, and he made mounts for shot against them, and engines:

text.DRB: And certain of them that were besieged, went forth: and some impious men of Israel joined themselves unto them,
text.KJV: Howbeit certaine of them that were besieged got forth, vnto whom some vngodly men of Israel ioyned themselues.

text.DRB: and they went to the king, & said: How long doest thou not judgements, & revengest our brethren?
text.KJV: And they went vnto the king and said, How long will it be ere thou execute iudgement, and auenge our brethren?

text.DRB: We decreed to serve thy father, and to walk in his precepts, and obey his edicts:
text.KJV: We haue beene willing to serue thy father, and to doe as he would haue vs, and to obey his commandements.

text.DRB: and the children of our people for this alienated themselves from us, & whosoever were found of us, they were slain, and our inheritances were spoiled.
text.KJV: For which cause they of our nation besiege the towre, and are alienated from vs: Moreouer as many of vs as they could light on, they slew, and spoiled our inheritance.

text.DRB: And not to us only have they extended the hand, but also into all our coasts.
text.KJV: Neither haue they stretched out their hand against vs only, but also against all their borders.

text.DRB: and behold they have approached this day to the castle in Jerusalem to take it, and they have fortified a fortress in Bethsura:
text.KJV: And behold this day are they besieging the towre at Ierusalem to take it: the Sanctuary also, and Bethsura haue they fortified.

text.DRB: and unless thou prevent them more speedily, they will do greater things than these, and thou shalt not be able to win them.
text.KJV: Wherefore if thou doest not preuent them quickly, they wil doe greater things then these, neither shalt thou be able to rule them.

text.DRB: And the king was angry, as he heard these things: & he called together all his friends, & the princes of his army, and them that were over the horsemen.
text.KJV: Now when the king heard this, he was angry, and gathered together all his friends, and the captaines of his armie, and those that had charge of the horse.

text.DRB: Yea and of other realms, and of the islands by the sea there came unto him hired armies.
text.KJV: There came also vnto him from other kingdomes, and from Isles of the Sea bands of hired souldiers.
So that the number of his armie was an hundred thousand foote men, and twentie thousand horsemen, and two and thirty Elephants exercised in battell.
text.NABRE: 6:30 His army numbered a hundred thousand footsoldiers, twenty thousand cavalry, and thirty-two elephants trained for war.
text.NRSV: The number of his forces was one hundred thousand infantry, twenty thousand cavalry, and thirty-two elephants accustomed to war.
text.DRB: And the number of his army was an hundred thousand footmen, and twenty thousand horsemen, and elephants thirty two, taught to battle.
And they came through Idumea, and approached to Bethsura, and fought many days, and they made engines and came forth, and burnt them with fire, and fought manfully.
text.KJV: These went through Idumea, and pitched against Bethsura which they assaulted many daies, making engines of warre: but they came out, and burnt them with fire, and fought valiantly.

text.DRB: And Judas departed from the castle, & removed the camp to Bethzacaram against the king's camp.
text.KJV: Upon this Iudas remoued from the towre, and pitched in Bathzacharias, ouer against the kings campe.

text.DRB: And the king arose before it was light, and stirred the bands into fierceness against the way to Bethzacaram: and the armies made themselves ready to the battle, and they sounded with trumpets:
text.KJV: Then the king rising very earely marched fiercely with his host toward Bathzacharias, where his armies made them ready to battell, and sounded the trumpets.

text.DRB: and to the elephants they shewed the blood of the grape, and of the mulberry, to provoke them to the battle.
text.KJV: And to the end they might prouoke the elephants to fight, they shewed them the blood of grapes & mulberies.

text.DRB: And they divided the beasts by the legions: & there stood by every elephant a thousand men in coats of mail, and helmets of brass on their heads: and five hundred horsemen set in order were chosen for every beast.
text.KJV: Moreouer, they diuided the beasts among the armies, and for euery elephant they appointed a thousand men, armed with coats of male, and with helmets of brasse on their heads, and besides this, for euery beast were ordained fiue hundred horsemen of the best.

text.DRB: These before the time wheresoever the beast was, there were they: & whither soever it went, they went, & they departed not from it.
text.KJV: These were ready at euery occasion: wheresoeuer the beast was, and whithersoeuer þ beast went, they went also, neither departed they from him.

text.DRB: Yea and upon them were strong wooden towers protecting every beast: and upon them engines, and upon every one thirty two men of strength which fought from above: and within the master of the beast.
text.KJV: And vpon the beastes were there strong towres of wood, which couered euery one of them, and were girt fast vnto them with deuices: there were also vpon euery one two and thirtie strong men that fought vpon them, besides the Indian that ruled him.

text.DRB: And the residue of the horsemen he placed on this side and on that side into two parts, with trumpets to stir up the army, and to urge them that stood thick together in the legions thereof.
text.KJV: As for the remnant of the horsemen they set them on this side, and that side, at the two parts of the host giuing them signes what to do, and being harnessed all ouer amidst the rankes.

text.DRB: And as the sun did shine upon the bucklers of gold, and of brass, the mountains glistered therewith, & they glistered as lamps of fire.
text.KJV: Now when the Sunne shone vpon the shields of golde, and brasse, the mountaines glistered therewith, and shined like lampes of fire.

text.DRB: And part of the king's army was severed by the high mountains, & another part by the low places: & they marched warily and orderly.
text.KJV: So part of the kings armie being spred vpon the high mountaines, and part on the valleyes below, they marched on safely, and in order.

text.DRB: And all the inhabitants of the land were moved at the voice of the multitude of them, and the marching of the troop, and the rattling of the armour, for it was an army exceeding great and strong.
text.KJV: Wherefore all that heard the noise of their multitude, and the marching of the company, and the ratling of the harnesse, were moued: for the army was very great and mighty.

text.DRB: And Judas approached, and his army into battle: and there fell of the king's army six hundred men.
text.KJV: Then Iudas and his host drew neere, and entred into battell, and there were slaine of the kings army, sixe hundred men.
¶ Eleazar also (syrnamed) Sauaran, perceiuing that one of the beasts, armed with royall harnesse, was higher then all the rest, and supposing that the king was vpon him,
text.NABRE: 6:43 Eleazar, called Avaran, saw one of the beasts covered with royal armor and bigger than any of the others, and so he thought the king was on it.
text.NRSV: And Eleazar, called Avaran, saw that one of the animals was equipped with royal armor. It was taller than all the others, and he supposed that the king was on it.
text.DRB: And Eleazar the son of Saura saw one of the beasts harnessed with the king's harness: & it was eminent above the other beasts; & it seemed to him that the king was on it:
and he gave himself to deliver his people, and to get himself an everlasting name.
text.KJV: Put himselfe in ieopardie, to the end hee might deliuer his people, and get him a perpetuall name:

text.DRB: And he ran to it boldly in the midst of the legion, killing on the right hand, & on the left, and they were slain of him on this side and that side.
text.KJV: Wherefore hee ranne vpon him courageously through the midst of the battell, slaying on the right hand, and on the left, so that they were diuided from him on both sides.
Which done, he crept vnder the Elephant, and thrust him vnder and slew him: whereupon the Elephant fell downe vpon him, and there he died.
text.NABRE: 6:46 He ran under the elephant, stabbed it and killed it. The beast fell to the ground on top of him, and he died there.
text.NRSV: He got under the elephant, stabbed it from beneath, and killed it; but it fell to the ground upon him and he died there.
text.DRB: And he went under the feet of the elephant, and put himself under him, and slew him: and it fell to the ground upon him, and he died there.
And they seeing the king's power, and the fierceness of his army, turned themselves aside from them.
text.KJV: How be it seeing the strength of the king, and the violence of his forces, turned away from them.

text.DRB: But the king's camp went up against them unto Jerusalem: and the king's camp approached to Jurie and mount Sion.
text.KJV: ¶ Then the kings armie went vp to Ierusalem to meet them, and the king pitched his tents against Iudea, and against mount Sion.

text.DRB: And he made peace with them that were in Bethsura: & they came forth out of the city, because there were no victuals for them there being shut up, because it was the sabbaths of the land.
text.KJV: But with them that were in Bethsura hee made peace: for they came out of the citie, because they had no victuals there, to endure the siege, it being a yeere of rest to the land.

text.DRB: And the king took Bethsura: and he placed there a garrison to keep it.
text.KJV: So the King tooke Bethsura, and set a garison there to keepe it.

text.DRB: And he turned the place of the sanctification many days: and he placed there arbalists, and engines, and instruments to cast fire, & pieces to cast stones, and arrows, & scorpions to shoot arrows, and slings.
text.KJV: As for the Sanctuarie hee besieged it many dayes: and set there artillerie with engins, and instruments to cast fire and stones, and pieces to cast darts, and slings.

text.DRB: And they also made engines against their engines, and they fought many days.
text.KJV: Whereupon they also made engins, against their engins, and helde them battell a long season.

text.DRB: But there were no victuals in the city, for that it was the seventh year: and they that had remained in Jury of the Gentiles, had consumed their remains, that had been laid up.
text.KJV: Yet at the last their vessels being without victuals, (for that it was the seuenth yeere, and they in Iudea that were deliuered from the Gentiles, had eaten vp the residue of the store)

text.DRB: And there remained in the holies few men, because famine had prevailed over them: and they were dispersed every man into his place.
text.KJV: There were but a few left in the Sanctuary, because the famine did so preuaile against them, that they were faine to disperse themselues, euery man to his owne place.

text.DRB: And Lysias heard that Philip, whom king Antiochus when he lived, had appointed to bring up his son to reign,
text.KJV: At that time Lysias heard say, that Philip (whom Antiochus the King whiles hee liued had appointed to bring vp his sonne Antiochus, that he might be king)

text.DRB: was returned from Persis, and Media, and the army that went with him, and that he sought to take upon him the affairs of the kingdom:
text.KJV: Was returned out of Persia, and Media, and the Kings host also that went with him, and that hee sought to take vnto him the ruling of the affaires.

text.DRB: and he hastened to go, and to say to the king, & to the princes of the army: We decay daily, and there is little victual for us, and the place that we besiege, is fenced, and it lieth upon us to take order for the kingdom.
text.KJV: Wherefore hee went in all haste, and said to the King, and the captaines of the host, and the company, Wee decay dayly, and our victuals are but small, and the place wee lay siege vnto is strong: and the affaires of the kingdome lie vpon vs.

text.DRB: Now therefore let us give the right hands to these men, and make peace with them, and with all their nation.
text.KJV: Now therefore let vs be friends with these men, and make peace with them, and with all their nation.

text.DRB: And let us decree for them, that they walk in their own ordinances as before. For, because of their ordinances which we despised, they have been wrath, and have done all these things.
text.KJV: And couenant with them, that they shall liue after their Lawes, as they did before: for they are therefore displeased, & haue done all these things because wee abolished their Lawes.

text.DRB: And the word was liked in the sight of the king, and of the princes: and he sent unto them to make peace: and they received it.
text.KJV: So the King and the Princes were content: wherefore hee sent vnto them to make peace, and they accepted thereof.

text.DRB: And the king and the princes sware to them: & they came out of the fortress.
text.KJV: Also the King and the Princes made an oath vnto them: whereupon they went out of the strong hold.

text.DRB: And the king entered mount Sion, and saw the munition of the place: and he brake quickly the oath which he sware, and commanded to destroy the wall round about.
text.KJV: Then the King entred into mount Sion, but when hee saw the strength of the place, hee brake his oath that hee had made, and gaue commandement to pull downe the wall round about.

text.DRB: And he departed in haste, and returned to Antioch, and found Philip ruling over the city: and he fought against him and won the city.
text.KJV: Afterward departed hee in all haste, and returned vnto Antiochia, where hee found Philip to bee master of the citie; So he fought against him, and tooke the citie by force.
In the hundreth and one and fiftieth yeere, Demetrius the sonne of Seleucus departed from Rome, and came vp with a fewe men vnto a citie of the Sea coast, and reigned there.
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 Expedition of Bacchides and Alcimus. In the one hundred and fifty-first year, Demetrius, son of Seleucus, set out from Rome, arrived with a few men at a coastal city, and began to rule there.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred fifty-first year Demetrius son of Seleucus set out from Rome, sailed with a few men to a town by the sea, and there began to reign.
text.DRB: In the year an hundreth fifty first went forth Demetrius the son of Seleucus from the city of Rome, and he ascended with few men into a city by the sea side, & reigned there.
And it came to pass, as he entered into the house of the kingdom of his fathers, the army took Antiochus, and Lysias, to bring them unto him.
text.KJV: And as he entred into the palace of his ancestors, so it was, that his forces had taken Antiochus and Lysias to bring them vnto him.

text.DRB: And the king was known to him: & he said: Shew me not their face.
text.KJV: Wherefore when he knew it, hee said; Let me not see their faces.

text.DRB: And the army slew them. And Demetrius sat upon the seat of his kingdom:
text.KJV: So his hoste slewe them. Now when Demetrius was set vpon the throne of his kingdome,
There came vnto him all the wicked and vngodly men of Israel, hauing Alcimus (who was desirous to be high Priest) for their captaine.
text.NABRE: 7:5 Then all the lawless men and renegades of Israel came to him. They were led by Alcimus, who desired to be high priest.
text.NRSV: Then there came to him all the lawless and ungodly men of Israel; they were led by Alcimus, who wanted to be high priest.
text.DRB: and there came to him the wicked and impious men of Israel: and Alcimus the captain of them, who would be made the Priest.
And they accused the people to the king, saying: Judas and his brethren have destroyed all thy friends, & us he hath destroyed out of our land.
text.KJV: And they accused the people to the king, saying; Iudas and his brethren haue slaine all thy friends, and driuen vs out of our owne land.

text.DRB: Now therefore send a man, whom thou doest credit, that he may go, and see all the destruction, that he hath done to us, and to the king's countries: and let him punish all his friends, & their aiders.
text.KJV: Now therefore send some man whom thou trustest, and let him goe and see what hauocke he hath made amongst vs, and in the kings land, and let him punish them with all them that aide them.

text.DRB: And the king chose of his friends Bacchides, that ruled beyond the great river in the kingdom, and was faithful to the king: & he sent him,
text.KJV: Then the king chose Bacchides a friend of the king, who ruled beyond the flood, and was a great man in the kingdome, and faithfull to the king.

text.DRB: to see the destruction that Judas hath made: and Alcimus the impious man he appointed to the Priesthood, and gave him in commandment to make revenge upon the children of Israel.
text.KJV: And him hee sent with that wicked Alcimus, whom hee made high Priest, and commanded that he should take vengeance of the children of Israel.

text.DRB: And they arose, and came with a great army into the land of Juda: and they sent messengers, and spake to Judas, and his brethren with peaceable words in guile.
text.KJV: So they departed, and came with a great power into the land of Iudea, where they sent messengers to Iudas and his brethren with peaceable words deceitfully.

text.DRB: And they attended not to their words: for they saw that they came with a great army.
text.KJV: But they gaue no heede to their words, for they sawe that they were come with a great power.

text.DRB: And there assembled to Alcimus and Bacchides a congregation of the scribes to require the things that are just:
text.KJV: Then did there assemble vnto Alcimus and Bacchides, a company of Scribes, to require iustice.

text.DRB: and first, the Assideans that were in the children of Israel, and they sought peace of them.
text.KJV: Now the Assideans were the first among the children of Israel, that sought peace of them:

text.DRB: for they said: A man that is a Priest of the seed of Aaron is come, he will not deceive us.
text.KJV: For, said they, one that is a Priest of the seede of Aaron, is come with this armie, and he will doe vs no wrong.

text.DRB: And he spake with them peaceable words: and he sware to them, saying: We will do you no harm, nor your friends.
text.KJV: So he spake vnto them peaceably, and sware vnto them, saying; We will procure the harme neither of you nor your friends.

text.DRB: And they believed him. And he took of them threescore men, & slew them in one day, according to the word that is written:
text.KJV: Whereupon they beleeued him: howbeit hee tooke of them threescore men, and slewe them in one day, according to the words which he wrote:

text.DRB: The flesh of thy saints, and the blood of them they have shed round about Jerusalem, and there was none to bury them.
text.KJV: The flesh of thy Saints and their blood haue they shed round about Ierusalem, and there was none to bury them.

text.DRB: And there fell fear, and trembling upon all the people, because they said: There is no truth and judgement in them: for they have transgressed the appointment & the oath which they sware.
text.KJV: Wherefore the feare and dread of them fell vpon all the people, who said, There is neither trueth, nor righteousnesse in them; for they haue broken the couenant and othe that they made.

text.DRB: And Bacchides removed the camp from Jerusalem, and approached to Bethzecha: and he sent, and took many of them that were fled from him, and certain of the people he killed, & threw them into a great pit.
text.KJV: After this remooued Bacchides from Ierusalem, and pitched his tents in Bezeth, where he sent and tooke many of the men that had forsaken him, and certaine of the people also, and when he had slaine them, into the great pit.

text.DRB: And he committed the country to Alcimus, and left with him aid to help him. And Bacchides went away to the king:
text.KJV: Then committed he the countrey to Alcimus, and left with him a power to aide him: so Bacchides went vnto the king.

text.DRB: & Alcimus did what he could for the principality of his Priesthood:
text.KJV: But Alcimus contended for the high Priesthood.

text.DRB: and there gathered together unto him all that disturbed their people, & they obtained the land of Juda, & made a great plague in Israel.
text.KJV: And vnto him resorted all such as troubled the people, who after they had gotten the land of Iuda into their power, did much hurt in Israel.

text.DRB: And Judas saw all the evils that Alcimus did, and they that were with him, to the children of Israel, much more than the Gentiles.
text.KJV: Now when Iudas saw all the mischiefe that Alcimus and his company had done among the Israelites, euen aboue the heathen,

text.DRB: And he went out into all the coasts of Jurie round about, & did vengeance upon the men that revolted, & they ceased to go forth any more into the country.
text.KJV: He went out into all the coast of Iudea round about, and tooke vengeance of them that had reuolted from him, so that they durst no more goe foorth into the countrey.

text.DRB: But Alcimus saw that Judas prevailed, & they that were with him: & he knew that he could not stand with them, and he went back to the king, & accused them of many crimes.
text.KJV: On the other side, when Alcimus saw that Iudas and his company had gotten the vpper hand, and knew that he was not able to abide their force, he went againe to the king, and said all the worst of them that he could.
Then the king sent Nicanor one of his honourable princes, a man that bare deadly hate vnto Israel, with commandement to destroy the people.
text.NABRE: 7:26 Defeat of Nicanor. Then the king sent Nicanor, one of his honored officers, who was a bitter enemy of Israel, with orders to destroy the people.
text.NRSV: Then the king sent Nicanor, one of his honored princes, who hated Israel and was its enemy, with orders to destroy the people.
text.DRB: And the king sent Nicanor, one of his nobler princes: that practised enmities against Israel: and he commanded him to destroy the people.
And Nicanor came into Jerusalem with a great army, and he sent to Judas & to his brethren in peaceable words with guile,
text.KJV: So Nicanor came to Ierusalem with a great force: and sent vnto Iudas and his brethren deceitfully with friendly words, saying,

text.DRB: saying: Let there be no fighting between me and you: I will come with few men, to see your faces with peace.
text.KJV: Let there be no battell betweene me and you, I will come with a fewe men, that I may see you in peace.

text.DRB: And he came to Judas, & they saluted one another peaceably: & the enemies were ready to catch Judas.
text.KJV: He came therefore to Iudas, and they saluted one another peaceably. Howbeit the enemies were prepared to take away Iudas by violence.

text.DRB: And the thing was known to Judas that he was come with guile: and he was sore afraid of him, and would not see his face any more.
text.KJV: Which thing after it was knowen to Iudas (to wit) that he came vnto him with deceit, he was sore afraid of him, and would see his face no more.

text.DRB: And Nicanor knew that his counsel was disclosed: and he went forth to Judas into battle beside Capharsalama.
text.KJV: Nicanor also when he saw that his counsell was discouered, went out to fight against Iudas besides Capharsalama.

text.DRB: And there fell of Nicanor's army almost five thousand men, & they fled into the city of David.
text.KJV: Where there were slaine of Nicanors side, about fiue thousand men, and fled into the citie of Dauid.

text.DRB: And after these words Nicanor went up into mount Sion: and there went forth of the Priests of the people to salute him in peace, and to shew him the holocausts, that were offered for the king.
text.KJV: After this went Nicanor vp to mount Sion, and there came out of the Sanctuarie certaine of the priestes, and certaine of the elders of the people to salute him peaceably, and to shewe him the burnt sacrifice that was offred for the king.

text.DRB: And scorning he contemned them, & polluted them: and he spake proudly,
text.KJV: But he mocked them, and laughed at them, and abused them shamefully, and spake proudly,

text.DRB: and sware with anger, saying: Unless Judas be delivered, & his army into my hands, incontinent when I shall return in peace I will burn this house. And he went out with great anger:
text.KJV: And swore in his wrath, saying, vnlesse Iudas and his hoste be now deliuered into my hands, if euer I come againe in safetie, I will burne vp this house: and with that he went out in a great rage.

text.DRB: and the Priests entered in, and stood before the face of the altar and the temple: and weeping they said:
text.KJV: Then the priests entred in, and stood before the altar, and the Temple, weeping, and saying,

text.DRB: Thou o Lord hast chosen this house for thy name to be invocated therein, that it might be a house of prayer, and obsecration for thy people.
text.KJV: Thou O Lord didst choose this house, to be called by thy Name, and to be a house of prayer and petition for thy people.

text.DRB: Take vengeance upon this man, and his army, and let them fall by the sword: remember their blasphemies, and grant not unto them to be permanent.
text.KJV: Be auenged of this man and his hoste, and let them fall by the sword: Remember their blasphemies, and suffer them not to continue any longer.

text.DRB: And Nicanor went out from Jerusalem, & moved the camp, near to Bethhoron: and the army of Syria met him.
text.KJV: So Nicanor went out of Ierusalem, & pitched his tents in Bethoron, where an hoste out of Syria met him.

text.DRB: And Judas approached in Adarsa with three thousand men: and Judas prayed, and said:
text.KJV: But Iudas pitched in Adasa with three thousand men, and there he prayed, saying,

text.DRB: They that were sent by king Sennacherib, o Lord, because they blasphemed thee, an angel came forth, and struck of them an hundred eighty five thousand:
text.KJV: O Lord, when they that were sent from the king of the Assyrians blasphemed, thine Angel went out, and smote a hundred, fourescore, and fiue thousand of them.

text.DRB: so destroy this army in our sight today, and let the rest know that he hath spoken it upon the holy places: and judge thou him according to his malice.
text.KJV: Euen so destroy thou this host before vs this day, that the rest may know that he hath spoken blasphemously against thy Sanctuary, and iudge thou him according to his wickednesse.
So the thirteenth day of the moneth Adar, the hostes ioyned battell, but Nicanors host was discomfited, & he himselfe was first slaine in the battell.
text.NABRE: 7:43 The armies met in battle on the thirteenth day of the month Adar. Nicanor’s army was crushed, and he himself was the first to fall in the battle.
text.NRSV: So the armies met in battle on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar. The army of Nicanor was crushed, and he himself was the first to fall in the battle.
text.DRB: And the armies joined battle the thirteenth day of the month Adar: and the camp of Nicanor was discomfited, & himself was slain first in the battle.
And as his army saw that Nicanor was slain, they threw away their weapons, & fled:
text.KJV: Now when Nicanors host saw that he was slaine, they cast away their weapons, and fled.

text.DRB: and they pursued them one day's journey from Adazer, even till ye come into Gazara, & with trumpets they sounded after them with significations.
text.KJV: Then they pursued after them a dayes iourney from Adasa, vnto Gasera, sounding an alarme after them with their trumpets.

text.DRB: And they went forth out of all the towns of Jurie round about, and they pushed them with the horns, and they turned again to them, and they were all slain with the sword, and there was not left of them so much as one.
text.KJV: Whereupon they came forth out of all the townes of Iudea round about, and closed them in, so that they turning backe vpon them that pursued them, were all slaine with the sword, and not one of them was left.
Afterwards they tooke þ spoiles, and the pray, and smote off Nicanors head, & his right hand, which he stretched out so proudly, and brought them away; and hanged them vp, towards Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 7:47 Then the Jews collected the spoils and the plunder; they cut off Nicanor’s head and his right arm, which he had lifted up so arrogantly. These they brought and displayed in the sight of Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: Then they took the spoils and the plunder; they cut off Nicanor’s head and the right hand that he had so arrogantly stretched out, and brought them and displayed them just outside Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And they took the spoils of them for a prey: and Nicanor's head they cut off, and his right hand, which he had proudly stretched forth, & they brought it, & hung it up against Jerusalem.
And the people rejoiced exceedingly, & they spent that day in great gladness.
text.KJV: For this cause the people reioyced greatly, and they kept that day, a day of great gladnesse.

text.DRB: And he ordained that this day should be kept every year the thirteenth of the month Adar.
text.KJV: Moreouer they ordeined to keepe yeerely this day, being the thirteenth of Adar.

text.DRB: And the land of Juda was quiet for a few days.
text.KJV: Thus the land of Iuda was in rest a litle while.

Now Iudas had heard of the fame of the Romanes, that they were mighty and valiant men, and such as would louingly accept all that ioyned themselues vnto them, and make a league of amitie with all that came vnto them,
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 Eulogy of the Romans. Judas had heard of the reputation of the Romans. They were valiant fighters and acted amiably to all who took their side. They established a friendly alliance with all who applied to them.
text.NRSV: Now Judas heard of the fame of the Romans, that they were very strong and were well-disposed toward all who made an alliance with them.
text.DRB: And Judas heard of the name of the Romans, that they are mighty of power, and agree unto all things that are requested of them: and whosoever have come unto them, they have made amity with them, and that they are mighty of power.
And they heard of their battles, and goodly acts, which they did in Galatia, that they overcame them, and brought them under tribute:
text.KJV: And that they were men of great valour: It was told him also of their warres and noble acts which they had done amongst the Galatians, and how they had conquered them, and brought them vnder tribute.

text.DRB: and how great things they did in the country of Spain, & that they brought into their power the metals of silver and gold, that are there, and possessed every place by their counsel and patience:
text.KJV: And what they had done in þ countrey of Spaine, for the winning of the mines of the siluer & gold which is there

text.DRB: and destroyed the places that were very far off from them, and the kings that came upon them from the ends of the earth, and struck them with a great plague: and the rest give them tribute every year.
text.KJV: And that by their policie and patience, they had conquered all that place (though it were very farre from them) and the kings also that came against them from the vttermost part of the earth, till they had discomfited them, & giuen them a great ouerthrow, so that the rest did giue them tribute euery yere.

text.DRB: And Philip and Perses the king of the Ceteans, & the rest that had borne weapon against them, they discomfited in battle, and overcame them:
text.KJV: Besides this, how they had discomfited in battell Philip, and Perseus king of the Citims, with others that lift vp themselues against them, and had ouercome them.

text.DRB: and that Antiochus the great king of Asia, who made battle against them, having an hundred and twenty elephants, and horsemen, and chariots and army exceeding great was discomfited by them:
text.KJV: How also Antiochus the great king of Asia that came against them in battaile, hauing an hundred and twentie Elephants with horsemen and chariots, and a very great armie, was discomfited by them.

text.DRB: and that they took him alive, and appointed him that himself and they that should reign after him, should give a great tribute, and he should give hostages, and the thing appointed him.
text.KJV: And how they tooke him aliue, and couenanted that hee and such as reigned after him, should pay a great tribute, and giue hostages, and that which was agreed vpon,

text.DRB: and the region of the Indes, and the Medes, and the Lydians of their best countries: and the same being taken of them, they gave to Eumenes the king.
text.KJV: And the country of India, and Media, and Lidia, and of the goodliest countries: which they tooke of him, and gaue to king Eumenes.

text.DRB: And that they which were in Hellada, would have gone to dispatch them: and the word was known to them,
text.KJV: Moreouer how the Grecians had determined to come and destroy them.

text.DRB: and they sent unto them one captain, and they fought against them, and many of them were slain, and they led away their wives captive, and their children, and spoiled them, and possessed their land, and destroyed their walls, and brought them into bondage even until this day:
text.KJV: And that they hauing knowledge thereof sent against them a certaine captaine, and fighting with them slew many of them, and caried away captiues, their wiues, and their children, and spoiled them, and tooke possession of their lands, and pulled downe their strong holds, and brought them to be their seruants vnto this day.

text.DRB: and the residue of kingdoms, and isles that some time had resisted them, they spoiled, and brought under their power.
text.KJV: how they destroyed and brought vnder their dominion, all other kingdomes and isles that at any time resisted them.

text.DRB: But with their friends, and those that rested in them, they kept amity, and obtained the kingdoms, that were next, and that were far off: that whosoever heard their name, feared them.
text.KJV: But with their friends, and such as relied vpon them they kept amitie: and that they had conquered kingdomes both farre and nigh, insomuch as all that heard of their name were afraid of them.

text.DRB: But such as they would help to reign, those reigned: and whom they would, they deposed from the kingdom: and they were exalted exceedingly.
text.KJV: Also that whom they would helpe to a kingdome, those raigne, and whom againe they would, they displace: finally that they were greatly exalted.

text.DRB: And in all these none bare a crown, nor was clothed with purple, to be magnified therein.
text.KJV: Yet for all this, none of them wore a crowne, or was clothed in purple to be magnified thereby.

text.DRB: And that they made themselves a court, and consulted daily three hundred and twenty, that sat in counsel always for the multitude, that they might do the things that were convenient:
text.KJV: Moreouer how they had made for themselues a senate house, wherin three hundred and twentie men sate in counsell daily, consulting alway for the people, to the end they might be wel ordered

text.DRB: and they commit their government to one man every year to rule over all their land, and all they obey one, and there is no envy, nor emulation amongst them.
text.KJV: And that they committed their gouernment to one man euery yeere, who ruled ouer all their countrie, and that all were obedient to that one, and that there was neither enuy, nor emulation amongst them.

In consideration of these things Iudas chose Eupolemus the sonne of Iohn, the sonne of Accas, and Iason the sonne of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to make a league of amitie and confederacie with them,
text.NABRE: 8:17 Treaty with the Romans. So Judas chose Eupolemus, son of John, son of Accos, and Jason, son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance with them.
text.NRSV: So Judas chose Eupolemus son of John son of Accos, and Jason son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance,
text.DRB: And Judas chose Eupolemus the son of John, the son of Jacob, and Jason the son of Eleazar, & he sent them to Rome to make amity and society with them:

text.KJV: that they would take the yoke from them, for they saw that the kingdome of the Grecians did oppresse Israel with seruitude
text.NABRE: 8:18 He did this to lift the yoke from Israel, for it was obvious that the kingdom of the Greeks was subjecting them to slavery.
text.NRSV: and to free themselves from the yoke; for they saw that the kingdom of the Greeks was completely enslaving Israel.
text.DRB: and that they should take from them the yoke of the Greeks, because they saw that they pressed the kingdom of Israel unto bondage.
And they went to Rome, a way exceeding great, and they entered into the court, and said:
text.KJV: They went therefore to Rome (which was a very great iourney) and came into the Senate, where they spake and said,

text.DRB: Judas Machabeus, and his brethren, and the people of the Jews have sent us unto you to make society and peace with you, and to write us your fellows and friends.
text.KJV: Iudas Maccabeus with his brethren, and the people of the Iewes, haue sent vs vnto you, to make a confederacie, and peace with you, and that we might be registred, your confederats and friends.

text.DRB: And the word was liked in their sight.
text.KJV: So that matter pleased the Romanes well.
And this is the copie of the Epistle which (the Senate) wrote backe againe, in tables of brasse: and sent to Ierusalem, that there they might haue by them a memorial of peace & confederacy.
text.NABRE: 8:22 and this is a copy of the reply they inscribed on bronze tablets and sent to Jerusalem, to remain there with the Jews as a record of peace and alliance:
text.NRSV: This is a copy of the letter that they wrote in reply, on bronze tablets, and sent to Jerusalem to remain with them there as a memorial of peace and alliance:
text.DRB: And this is the rescript that they wrote again in tables of brass, and sent into Jerusalem, that it might be with them there a memorial of peace and society.
BE IT Well to The Romans, and to the nation of the Jews by sea, and by land for ever: and sword and enemy be far from them.
text.KJV: Good successe be to the Romans and to the people of the Iewes, by Sea, and by land for euer: the sword also and enemie, be farre from them.

text.DRB: But and if war be toward the Romans first, or all their fellows in all their dominion:
text.KJV: If there come first any warre vpon the Romans or any of their confederats throughout all their dominion,

text.DRB: the nation of the Jews shall give aid, according as the time shall appoint, with full heart:
text.KJV: The people of the Iewes shall helpe them, as the time shall be appointed, with all their heart.

text.DRB: and to them fighting, they shall not give nor allow wheat, armour, money, ships, as it hath pleased the Romans: & they shall keep their charge, taking nothing of them.
text.KJV: Neither shal they giue any thing, vnto them that make war vpon them, or aide them with victuals, weapons, money, or ships, as it hath seemed good vnto the Romans, but they shall keepe their couenant without taking any thing therefore.

text.DRB: In like manner also & if war shall happen first to the nation of the Jews, the Romans shall help with all their heart, according as the time shall permit them:
text.KJV: In the same maner also, if warre come first vpon the nation of the Iewes, the Romans shall helpe them with all their heart, according as the time shall be appointed them.

text.DRB: & to them helping shall not be given wheat, armour, money, ships, as it hath pleased the Romans: and they shall keep their charge without guile.
text.KJV: Neither shal victuals be giuen to thē that take part against thē, or weapons, or money, or ships, as it hath seemed good to the Romanes; but they shall keepe their couenants, and that without deceit.

text.DRB: According to these words did the Romans agree to the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: According to these articles did the Romanes make a couenant with the people of the Iewes.

text.DRB: And if after these words these or they will add to these, or take away any thing, they shall do it at their pleasure: and whatsoever they shall add, or take away, shall be ratified.
text.KJV: Howbeit, if hereafter the one partie or the other, shall thinke meete to adde or diminish any thing, they may doe it at their pleasures, and whatsoeuer they shall adde or take away, shalbe ratified.

text.DRB: Yea and concerning the evils, that Demetrius the king hath done against them, we have written to him, saying: Why hast thou aggravated the yoke upon our friends and fellows the Jews?
text.KJV: And as touching the euils that Demetrius doeth to the Iewes, wee haue written vnto him, saying, Wherefore hast thou made thy yoke heauie vpon our friends, and confederats the Iewes?

text.DRB: If therefore they come again unto us against thee, we will do judgement for them, and will fight with thee by sea and land.
text.KJV: If therefore they complaine any more against thee: wee will doe them iustice, and fight with thee by sea and by land.

Furthermore, when Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his hoste were slaine in battell, hee sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Iudea the second time, and with them the chiefe strength of his hoste.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 Death of Judas. When Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his army had fallen in battle, he again sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judah, along with the right wing of his army.
text.NRSV: When Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his army had fallen in battle, he sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judah a second time.
text.DRB: In the mean time as Demetrius heard that Nicanor was fallen and his army in the battle, he added to send Bacchides and Alcimus again into Jurie, and the right wing with them.
And they went the way, that leadeth into Galgal, and camped in Masaloth, which is in Arbellis: and they won it, and slew of men many souls.
text.KJV: Who went forth by the way that leadeth to Galgala, and pitched their tents before Masaloth, which is in Arbela, and after they had wonne it, they slew much people.

text.DRB: In the first month of the year an hundreth and fifty two they brought the army near to Jerusalem:
text.KJV: Also the first moneth of the hundred fiftie and second yeere, they encamped before Ierusalem.

text.DRB: and they arose, and went into Beræa twenty thousand men, and two thousand horsemen.
text.KJV: From whence they remoued and went to Berea, with twentie thousand footmen, and two thousand horsemen.

text.DRB: And Judas had camped in Laisa, and three thousand chosen men with him:
text.KJV: Now Iudas had pitched his tents at Eleasa, and three thousand chosen men with him.

text.DRB: and they saw the multitude of the army that they were many, and they feared exceedingly: and many withdrew themselves out of the camp, and there remained of them but eight hundred men.
text.KJV: Who seeing the multitude of the other army to be so great, were sore afraide, whereupon many conueyed themselues out of the hoste, insomuch as there abode of them no moe but eight hundred men.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that his army shrunk away, and the battle pressed upon him, and his heart was broken: because he had not time to gather them together, and he was discouraged.
text.KJV: When Iudas therefore saw that his hoste slipt away, and that the battell pressed vpon him, he was sore troubled in mind, and much distressed for that he had no time to gather them together.

text.DRB: And he said to them that were remaining: Let us rise, and go to our adversaries, if we may be able to fight against them.
text.KJV: Neuerthelesse vnto them that remained, he said; Let vs arise and goe vp against our enemies, if peraduenture we may be able to fight with them.

text.DRB: And they dissuaded him saying: We are not able, but let us save our lives now, and return to our brethren, and then we will fight against them: and we are few.
text.KJV: But they dehorted him, saying, Wee shall neuer be able: Let vs now rather saue our liues, and hereafter we will returne with our brethren, and fight against them: for we are but few.

text.DRB: And Judas said: God forbid we should do this thing, to fly from them: & if our time be near, let us die manfully for our brethren, and let us not stain our glory.
text.KJV: Then Iudas said, God forbid that I should doe this thing, and flee away from them: If our time be come, let vs die manfully for our brethren, and let vs not staine our honour.

With that the hoste remoued out of their tents, and stood ouer against them, their horsemen being diuided into two troupes, and their slingers and archers going before the hoste, and they that marched in the foreward were all mighty men.
text.NABRE: 9:11 Then the army of Bacchides moved out of camp and took its position for combat. The cavalry were divided into two squadrons, and the slingers and the archers came on ahead of the army, and in the front line were all the best warriors. Bacchides was on the right wing.
text.NRSV: The army of Bacchides marched out from the camp and took its stand for the encounter.
text.DRB: And the army moved out of the camp, and they stood to meet them: and the horsemen were divided into two parts, and the slingers, and the archers went before the army, & of the forward all mighty.
And Bacchides was in the right wing, and the legion approached on two sides, & they sounded with trumpets:
text.KJV: As for Bacchides, hee was in the right wing, so the hoste drew neere on the two parts, and sounded their trumpets.

text.DRB: and they also cried out that were on Judas' side, even they also, & the earth was moved at the voice of the armies: and the battle was fought from morning even until the evening.
text.KJV: They also of Iudas side, euen they sounded their trumpets also, so that the earth shooke at the noise of the armies, and the battell continued from morning till night.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that the stronger part of the army was on the right hand, and all the stout of heart came together with him:
text.KJV: Now when Iudas perceiued that Bacchides and the strength of his armie were on the right side, he tooke with him all the hardy men,

text.DRB: and the right side was discomfited of them, & he pursued them even to the mount of Azotus.
text.KJV: Who discomfited the right wing, and pursued them vnto the mount Azotus.

text.DRB: And they that were in the left wing, saw that the ring wing was discomfited, and they followed after Judas, and them that were with him at the back:
text.KJV: But when they of the left wing, saw that they of the right wing were discomfited, they followed vpon Iudas and those that were with him hard at the heeles from behinde:

text.DRB: and the battle grew sore, and there fell many wounded of these and of them.
text.KJV: Whereupon there was a sore battell, insomuch as many were slaine on both parts.
Iudas also was killed, and the remnant fled.
text.NABRE: 9:18 Then Judas fell, and the rest fled.
text.NRSV: Judas fell, and the rest fled.
text.DRB: And Judas was slain, and the rest fled.

text.KJV: Then Ionathan and Simon tooke Iudas their brother, and buried him in the sepulchre of his fathers in Modin.
text.NABRE: 9:19 Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein,
text.DRB: And Jonathas and Simon took Judas their brother, and buried him in the sepulchre of their fathers in the city Modin.

text.KJV: Moreouer they bewailed him, and all Israel made great lamentation for him, and mourned many dayes, saying;
text.NABRE: 9:20 All Israel wept for him with great lamentation. They mourned for him many days, and they said,
text.NRSV: and wept over him. And all Israel made great lamentation for him; they mourned many days.
text.DRB: And all the people of Israel lamented him with great lamentation, and they mourned many days,
and said: How is the mighty fallen, that saved the people of Israel!
text.KJV: How is the valiant man fallen, that deliuered Israel?

text.DRB: And the rest of the words of Judas' battles, and of the valiant acts that he did, and of his greatness are not described: for they were exceeding many.
text.KJV: As for the other things concerning Iudas and his warres, and the noble actes which he did, and his greatnesse, they are not written: for they were very many.

text.DRB: And it came to pass: after the death of Judas, there came forth the wicked men in all the coasts of Israel, & there arose all that wrought iniquity.
text.KJV: ¶ Now after the death of Iudas, the wicked began to put foorth their heads in all the coasts of Israel, and there rose vp all such as wrought iniquitie.

text.DRB: In those days was made an exceeding great famine, and all their country with themselves yielded to Bacchides.
text.KJV: In those dayes also was there a very great famine, by reason whereof the countrey reuolted, and went with them.

text.DRB: And Bacchides chose the impious men, & appointed them lords of the country:
text.KJV: Then Bacchides chose the wicked men, and made them lordes of the countrey.

text.DRB: and they sought out, & searched for the friends of Judas, & brought them to Bacchides, and he took revenge on them, and scorned them.
text.KJV: And they made enquirie & search for Iudas friends, and brought them vnto Bacchides, who tooke vengeance of them, and vsed them despitefully.

text.DRB: And there was made great tribulation in Israel, such as was not since the day, that there was no Prophet seen in Israel.
text.KJV: So was there a great affliction in Israel, the like whereof was not since the time that a Prophet was not seene amongst them.
For this cause all Iudas friends came together, & said vnto Ionathan,
text.NABRE: 9:28 Then all the friends of Judas came together and said to Jonathan:
text.NRSV: Then all the friends of Judas assembled and said to Jonathan:
text.DRB: And all the friends of Judas gathered, & said to Jonathas:
Since thy brother Judas died, there is not a man like unto him, to go forth against the enemies, Bacchides, & them that are the enemies of our nation.
text.KJV: Since thy brother Iudas died, we haue no man like him to goe foorth against our enemies, and Bacchides, and against them of our nation that are aduersaries to vs.

text.DRB: Now therefore thee have we chosen this day to be for him our prince, and captain to wage our battle.
text.KJV: Now therefore wee haue chosen thee this day to be our prince, and captaine in his stead, that thou mayest fight our battels.
Upon this, Ionathan tooke the gouernance vpon him at that time, and rose vp in stead of his brother Iudas.
text.NABRE: 9:31 From that moment Jonathan accepted the leadership, and took the place of Judas his brother.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan accepted the leadership at that time in place of his brother Judas.
text.DRB: And Jonathas took upon him at that time the princedom, & arose in the place of Judas his brother.
And Bacchides understood it, and sought to kill him.
text.KJV: But when Bacchides gat knowledge thereof, he sought for to slay him.

text.DRB: And Jonathas understood it, and Simon his brother, and all that were with them: and they fled into the desert of Thecua, and they pitched by the water of the lake Asphar.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan and Simon his brother, and all that were with him, perceiuing that, fled into the wildernes of Thecoe, and pitched their tents by the water of the poole Asphar.

text.DRB: And Bacchides understood it, and in the day of the Sabbaths came himself, and all his army over Jordan.
