text.KJV: Which when Bacchides vnderstood, he came neere to Iordan with all his hoste vpon the Sabbath day.

text.DRB: And Jonathas sent his brother captain of the people, to desire the Nabutheians his friends, that they would lend him their provision, which was copious.
text.KJV: Now Ionathan had sent his brother a captaine of the people, to pray his friendes the Nabbathites that they might leaue with them their cariage, which was much.

text.DRB: And the children of Jambri issued forth of Madaba, and took John, & all things that he had, and went away having them.
text.KJV: But the children of Iambri came out of Medaba, and tooke Iohn and all that hee had, and went their way with it.

text.DRB: After these words, it was told Jonathas and Simon his brother, that the children of Jambri make a great marriage, & bring the bride out of Madaba, the daughter of one of the great princes of Chanaan with great pomp.
text.KJV: After this came word to Ionathan and Simon his brother, that the children of Iambri made a great mariage, and were bringing the bride from Nadabatha with a great traine, as being the daughter of one of the great princes of Canaan.

text.DRB: And they remembered the blood of John their brother: and they went up, and hid themselves under the covert of the mountain.
text.KJV: Therfore they remembred Iohn their brother, and went vp and hidde themselues vnder the couert of the mountaine.

text.DRB: And they lifted up their eyes, and saw: and lo a tumult, a great preparation: and the bridegroom came forth, and his friends, and his brethren to meet them with timbrels, and musical instruments, and many weapons.
text.KJV: Where they lift vp their eyes, and looked, & behold, there was much adoe and great cariage: and the bridegrome came foorth, and his friends & brethren to meet them with drums and instruments of musicke, and many weapons.

text.DRB: And they rose upon them out of the embushment, and slew them, and there fell many wounded, & the rest fled into the mountains, and they took all the spoils of them:
text.KJV: Then Ionathan and they that were with him, rose vp against them from the place where they lay in ambush, and made a slaughter of them in such sort, as many fell downe dead, and the remnant fledde into the mountaine, and they tooke all their spoiles.

text.DRB: and the marriage was turned into mourning, and the voice of their musical instruments into lamentation.
text.KJV: Thus was the mariage turned into mourning, and the noise of their melody into lamentation.

text.DRB: And they took revenge of the blood of their brother: and they returned to the bank of Jordan.
text.KJV: So when they had auenged fully the blood of their brother, they turned againe to the marish of Iordan.

text.DRB: And Bacchides heard it, and he came on the day of the Sabbaths even to the brink of Jordan with a great power:
text.KJV: Now when Bacchides heard hereof, hee came on the Sabbath day vnto the banks of Iordan with a great power.

text.DRB: And Jonathas said to his company: Let us arise, and fight against our enemies: for it is not today as yesterday, and the day before.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan sayde to his company, Let vs goe vp now and fight for our liues, for it standeth not with vs to day, as in time past:

text.DRB: For lo battle directly against us, and the water of Jordan on this side and on that side, and banks, and marishes, & forests: and there is no place to turn aside.
text.KJV: For behold, the battell is before vs and behinde vs, and the water of Iordan on this side and that side, the marish likewise and wood, neither is there place for vs to turne aside.

text.DRB: Now therefore cry ye unto heaven, that you may be delivered out of the hand of your enemies. And battle was joined.
text.KJV: Wherefore cry ye now vnto heauen, that ye may be deliuered from the hand of your enemies.

text.DRB: And Jonathas stretched forth his hand to strike Bacchides, & he turned aside from him backwards.
text.KJV: With that they ioyned battel, and Ionathan stretched foorth his hand to smite Bacchides, but hee turned backe from him.

text.DRB: And Jonathas lept aside, and they that were with him into Jordan, & they swam over Jordan to them:
text.KJV: Then Ionathan and they that were with him, leapt into Iordan, and swamme ouer vnto the farther banke: howbeit the other passed not ouer Iordan vnto them.

text.DRB: and there fell of Bacchides' part that day a thousand men: and they returned into Jerusalem,
text.KJV: So there were slaine of Bacchides side that day about a thousand men

text.DRB: and built fenced cities in Jurie, the fortress that was in Jericho, and in Ammaum, and in Bethoron, and in Bethel, and Thamnata, and Phara, and Thopo with high walls, and gates, & locks.
text.KJV: Afterward returned to Ierusalem, and repaired the strong cities in Iudea: the fort in Iericho, and Emmaus, and Bethoron, and Bethel, and Thamnatha, Pharathoni, and Taphon (these did he strengthen with high wals, with gates, & with barres.)

text.DRB: And he placed a garrison in them, that they might exercise enmities against Israel:
text.KJV: And in them he set a garison, that they might worke malice vpon Israel.

text.DRB: and he fenced the city Bethsura, and Gazara, and the castle, and set garrisons in them, & provision of victuals:
text.KJV: He fortified also the citie Bethsura, and Gazara, and the towre, and put forces in them, and prouision of victuals.

text.DRB: & he took the sons of the princes of the country for hostages, and put them in the castle in Jerusalem into ward.
text.KJV: Besides, he tooke the chiefe mens sonnes in the country for hostages, and put them into the towre at Ierusalem to be kept.

Moreouer, in the hundred, fiftie and third yere, in the second moneth, Alcimus commanded that the wall of the inner court of the Sanctuarie should be pulled downe, he pulled downe also the works of the prophets.
text.NABRE: 9:54 In the one hundred and fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus ordered the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary to be torn down, thus destroying the work of the prophets. But he only began to tear it down.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus gave orders to tear down the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary. He actually began to tear down the work of the prophets!
text.DRB: And in the year an hundred fifty three, the second month, Alcimus commanded the walls of the inner holy house to be destroyed, and the works of the Prophets to be destroyed: & he began to destroy.

In that time was Alcimus strucken: and his works were stayed, and his mouth was stopped, and he was dissolved with the palsy, neither could he speak a word any more, and give commandment touching his house.
text.KJV: And as he began to pull downe, euen at that time was Alcimus plagued, and his enterprises hindered: for his mouth was stopped, and he was taken with a palsie, so that hee could no more speake any thing, nor giue order concerning his house.
So Alcimus died at that time with great torment.
text.NABRE: 9:56 Alcimus died in great agony at that time.
text.NRSV: He died at that time with great agony.
text.DRB: And Alcimus died at that time with great torment.
And Bacchides saw that Alcimus was dead: and he returned to the king, & the land was quiet for two years.
text.KJV: Now when Bacchides saw that Alcimus was dead, he returned to the king, wherupon the land of Iudea was in rest two yeere.

text.DRB: And all the wicked thought saying: Behold Jonathas, and they that are with him, dwell in silence securely: now therefore let us bring Bacchides, and he shall take them all in one night.
text.KJV: Then all the vngodly men held a counsell, saying, Behold, Ionathan and his companie are at ease, and dwell without care: now therefore wee will bring Bacchides hither, who shall take them all in one night.

text.DRB: And they went, and gave him counsel.
text.KJV: So they went, and consulted with him.

text.DRB: And he arose to come with a great army: and he sent secretly epistles to his fellows that were in Jurie, to take Jonathas, and them that were with him: but they could not, because their counsel was known to them.
text.KJV: Then remoued he, and came with a great hoste, and sent letters priuily to his adherents in Iudea, that they should take Ionathan, and those that were with him: Howbeit they could not, because their counsell was knowen vnto them.

text.DRB: And he apprehended of the men of the country, that were the principal of the mischief, fifty men, and he slew them.
text.KJV: Wherefore they tooke of the men of the countrey that were authours of that mischiefe, about fiftie persons, and slew them.

text.DRB: And Jonathas retired aside, and Simon, and they that were with him into Bethbessen, which is in the desert: and he built up the ruins thereof, and they fortified it.
text.KJV: Afterward Ionathan and Simon, and they that were with him, got them away to Bethbasi, which is in the wildernesse, and they repaired the decayes thereof, and made it strong.

text.DRB: And Bacchides understood it, and gathered together all his multitude, and sent word to them that were of Jurie.
text.KJV: Which thing when Bacchides knew, he gathered together all his host, and sent word to them that were of Iudea.

text.DRB: And he came, and camped above Bethbessen, and assaulted it many days, and made engines.
text.KJV: Then went he and laid siege against Bethbasi, & they fought against it a long season, and made engines of warre.

text.DRB: And Jonathas left Simon his brother in the city, and went forth into the country, & came with a number,
text.KJV: But Ionathan left his brother Simon in the citie, and went forth himselfe into the countrey, and with a certaine number went he forth.

text.DRB: and struck Odares, and his brethren, and the children of Phaseron in their tabernacles, and he began to slay, and to grow in forces.
text.KJV: And he smote Odonarkes and his brethren, and the children of Phasiron in their tent.

text.DRB: But Simon and they that were with him, went out of the city, and burnt the engines,
text.KJV: And when he began to smite them, and came vp with his forces, Simon and his company went out of the citie, and burnt vp the engines of warre,

text.DRB: and they fought against Bacchides, & he was discomfited by them: and they afflicted him exceedingly, because his counsel, and his conflict was in vain.
text.KJV: And fought against Bacchides, who was discomfited by them, and they afflicted him sore. For his counsell and trauaile was in vaine.

text.DRB: And being angry against the wicked men, that had given him counsel to come into their country, he slew many of them: but himself thought with the rest to depart into their country.
text.KJV: Wherefore he was very wroth at the wicked men that gaue him counsell to come into the countrey, insomuch as he slew many of them, and purposed to returne into his owne countrey.

text.DRB: And Jonathas understood it, and he sent unto him legates to make peace with him, and to render unto him the captives.
text.KJV: Whereof when Ionathan had knowledge, he sent ambassadours vnto him, to the end he should make peace with him, & deliuer them the prisoners.

text.DRB: And he took it gladly, and did according to his words, and sware that he would do him no harm all the days of his life.
text.KJV: Which thing hee accepted, and did according to his demaunds, and sware vnto him that hee would neuer doe him harme all the dayes of his life.

text.DRB: And he rendered unto him the captives, which he before had taken for a prey, out of the land of Juda: & returning he departed into his own country, and he added no more to come into their coasts.
text.KJV: When therefore hee had restored vnto him the prisoners that he had taken aforetime out of the land of Iudea, he returned and went his way into his owne land, neither came he any more into their borders.

text.DRB: And the sword ceased out of Israel: and Jonathas dwelt in Machmas, and Jonathas began there to judge the people, and he destroyed the impious out of Israel.
text.KJV: Thus the sword ceased from Israel: but Ionathan dwelt at Machmas, and began to gouerne the people, and he destroyed the vngodly men out of Israel.
In the hundreth & sixtieth yere, Alexander the sonne of Antiochus surnamed Epiphanes, went vp and tooke Ptolemais: for the people had receiued him, by meanes whereof he reigned there.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 Jonathan Becomes High Priest. In the one hundred and sixtieth year, Alexander Epiphanes, son of Antiochus, came up and took Ptolemais. They accepted him as king and he began to reign there.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred sixtieth year Alexander Epiphanes, son of Antiochus, landed and occupied Ptolemais.
text.DRB: And in the year an hundreth threescore came up Alexander the son of Antiochus, that was surnamed Noble: and he took Ptolemais, and they received him, and he reigned there.
And Demetrius the king heard it, and he gathered together an exceeding great army, and went forth to meet him into battle.
text.KJV: Now when king Demetrius heard thereof, he gathered together an exceeding great host, and went foorth against him to fight.

text.DRB: And Demetrius sent an epistle to Jonathas in peaceable words, to magnify him.
text.KJV: Moreouer Demetrius sent letters vnto Ionathan with louing wordes, so as he magnified him.

text.DRB: For he said: Let us prevent to make peace with him, before he make with Alexander against us:
text.KJV: For, said hee, Let vs first make peace with him before he ioyne with Alexander against vs.

text.DRB: for he will remember all the evils, that we have done against him, and against his brother, and against his nation.
text.KJV: Else he wil remember all the euils that we haue done against him, and against his brethren and his people.

text.DRB: And he gave him authority to gather an army, and to make weapons, and to be himself his fellow: and the hostages that were in the castle, he commanded to be delivered to him.
text.KJV: Wherefore he gaue him authority to gather together an host, and to prouide weapons that hee might aide him in battell: he commaunded also that the hostages that were in the towre, should be deliuered him.

text.DRB: And Jonathas came into Jerusalem, and read the epistles in the hearing of all the people, and of them that were in the castle.
text.KJV: Then came Ionathan to Ierusalem, and read the letters in the audience of all the people, and of them that were in the towre.

text.DRB: And they feared with great fear, because they heard that the king gave him authority to gather an army.
text.KJV: Who were sore afraid when they heard that the king had giuen him authoritie to gather together an host.

text.DRB: And the hostages were delivered to Jonathas, and he rendered them to their parents.
text.KJV: Whereupon they of the towre deliuered their hostages vnto Ionathan, & he deliuered them vnto their parents.

text.DRB: And Jonathas dwelt in Jerusalem, and began to build, & to renew the city.
text.KJV: This done, Ionathan setled himselfe in Ierusalem, and began to build and repaire the citie.

text.DRB: And he spake to them that made the works, that they should build the walls, and mount Sion round about with square stones for munition: and so they did.
text.KJV: And he commaunded the workemen to build the wals, and the mount Sion round about with square stones, for fortification, and they did so.

text.DRB: And the Aliens fled that were in the holds, which Bacchides had built:
text.KJV: Then the strangers that were in the fortresses which Bacchides had built, fled away:

text.DRB: and every man left his place, & departed into his own country:
text.KJV: Insomuch as euery man left his place, and went into his owne country.

text.DRB: only in Bethsura there remained some of them, that had forsaken the law, and the precepts of God: for this was for a refuge to them.
text.KJV: Onely at Bethsura certaine of those that had forsaken the law, and the commaundements remained still: for it was their place of refuge.

text.DRB: And Alexander the king heard of the promises, that Demetrius had promised to Jonathas: and they told him the battles, & the worthy acts that he did, and his brethren, and the labours, that they endured:
text.KJV: Now when king Alexander had heard what promises Demetrius had sent vnto Ionathan: when also it was told him of the battels and noble acts which he & his brethren had done, and of the paines that they had indured,

text.DRB: and he said: Why, shall we find any such man? And now we will make him a friend, and our fellow.
text.KJV: He said, Shal we find such another man? Now therefore we will make him our friend, and confederate.

text.DRB: And he wrote an epistle, and sent it to him according to these words, saying:
text.KJV: Upon this he wrote a letter and sent it vnto him according to these words, saying:
King Alexander to his brother Ionathan, sendeth greeting:
text.NABRE: 10:18 “King Alexander sends greetings to his brother Jonathan.
text.NRSV: “King Alexander to his brother Jonathan, greetings.”
text.DRB: King Alexander to his brother Jonathas greeting.
We have heard of thee, that thou art mighty of power, and art meet to be our friend:
text.KJV: We haue heard of thee, that thou art a man of great power, and meete to be our friend.

Wherefore now this day we ordaine thee to bee the high priest of thy nation, and to be called the kings friend, (and there withall he sent him a purple robe and a crowne of gold) to take our part, and keepe friendship with vs.
text.NABRE: 10:20 We have therefore appointed you today to be high priest of your nation; you are to be called the King’s Friend, and you are to look after our interests and preserve friendship with us.” He also sent him a purple robe and a crown of gold.
text.NRSV: “So now we have appointed you today to be the high priest of your nation; you are to be called the king’s friend.”
text.DRB: and now we make thee this day high Priest of thy nation, that thou be called the king's friend (& he sent him purple, and a crown of gold) & in our affairs thou be of one mind with us, and keep amity towards us.

text.KJV: So in the seuenth moneth of the hundreth and sixtieth yere, at the feast of the Tabernacles, Ionathan put on the holy robe, and gathered together forces, and prouided much armour.
text.NABRE: 10:21 Jonathan put on the sacred vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred and sixtieth year at the feast of Booths, and he gathered an army and procured many weapons.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan put on the holy vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred sixtieth year, at the festival of booths.
text.DRB: And Jonathas put on him the holy stole the seventh month, in the year an hundreth threescore in the solemn day of Scenopegia: and he gathered an army, and made very many weapons.
And Demetrius heard these words, and was exceeding sorry, and said:
text.KJV: Wherof when Demetrius heard, he was very sory, and said,

text.DRB: What is this that we have done, that Alexander hath prevented us to take the friendship of the Jews for his strength?
text.KJV: What haue we done that Alexander hath preuented vs, in making amity with the Iewes to strengthen himself?

text.DRB: I also will write to them words of request, and dignities, and gifts: that they may be with me to aid me.
text.KJV: I also will write vnto them words of encouragement dignities and gifts, that I may haue their ayde.

text.DRB: And he wrote to them in these words: King Demetrius to the nation of the Jews greeting.
text.KJV: He sent vnto him therefore, to this effect: King Demetrius vnto the people of the Iewes, sendeth greeting:

text.DRB: Because you have kept covenant towards us, and have continued in our amity, and have not joined to our enemies, we have heard of it, and are glad.
text.KJV: Whereas you haue kept couenants with vs, & continued in our friendship, not ioyning your selues with our enemies, we haue heard hereof, & are glad:

text.DRB: And now persevere still to keep fidelity towards us, & we will reward you with good things, for that you have done with us.
text.KJV: Wherefore now continue yee still to be faithful vnto vs, and we will well recompence you for the things you doe in our behalfe,

text.DRB: And we will remit you many charges, and will give you many gifts.
text.KJV: And will grant you many immunities, and giue you rewards.

text.DRB: And now I release you, and all the Jews from tributes, & I pardon you the prices of salt, & remit the crowns, & the thirds of the seed:
text.KJV: And now I doe free you, and for your sake I release all the Iewes from tributes, and from the customes of salt, and from crowne taxes,

text.DRB: and the half of the fruit of trees, which is my portion, I leave to you from this day forward, that it be not taken of the land of Juda, and of the three cities that are added thereto of Samaria, & Galilee from this day & for ever:
text.KJV: And frō that which appertaineth vnto me to receiue for the third part of the seed, and the halfe of the fruit of the trees, I release it from this day forth, so that they shall not be taken of the land of Iudea, nor of the three gouernments which are added thereunto out of the country of Samaria and Galile, from this day forth for euermore.

text.DRB: & let Jerusalem be holy, and free with the coasts thereof: & the tithes, & tributes be they to it.
text.KJV: Let Ierusalem also bee holy and free, with the borders thereof, both from tenths and tributes.

text.DRB: I release also the authority of the castle, that is in Jerusalem, and I give it to the high priest, to place therein men whomsoever he shall choose, that may keep it.
text.KJV: And as for the towre which is at Ierusalem, I yeeld vp my authoritie ouer it, and giue it to the high Priest, that he may set in it such men as he shall choose to keepe it.

text.DRB: And every soul of the Jews, that is captive from the land of Juda in all my kingdom, I dismiss free for nothing, that all may be discharged also from tributes of their cattle.
text.KJV: Moreouer I freely set at libertie euery one of the Iewes that were carried captiues out of the land of Iudea, into any part of my kingdome, and I will that all my officers remit the tributes, euen of their cattell.

text.DRB: And all the solemn days, & the sabbaths, & the new moons, & the days appointed, and three days before the solemn day, and three days after the solemn day let them all be of immunity & remission, to all the Jews that are in my kingdom:
text.KJV: Furthermore, I will that all the Feasts and Sabbaths, & New moones and solemne dayes, and the three dayes before the Feast, and the three dayes after the Feast, shall be all dayes of immunitie and freedom for all the Iewes in my realme.

text.DRB: & no man shall have power to do any thing, & to make businesses against any of them in any cause.
text.KJV: Also no man shall haue authoritie to meddle with them, or to molest any of them in any matter.

text.DRB: And let there be enrolled of the Jews in the king's army to the number of thirty thousand men: & allowance shall be given them as behoveth to all the king's bands, & of them there shall be ordained certain, that shall be in the fortresses of the great king:
text.KJV: that there be enrolled amongst the kings forces about thirtie thousand men of the Iewes, vnto whom pay shall be giuen as belongeth to all the kings forces.

text.DRB: and of these there shall be appointed over the affairs of the kingdom, that are done of trust, and let there be princes of them, and let them walk in their own laws, as the king hath commanded in the land of Juda.
text.KJV: And of them some shalbe placed in the kings strong holds, of whom also some shall be set ouer the affaires of the kingdome, which are of trust: and I will that their ouerseers and gouernours be of themselues, and that they liue after their owne lawes, euen as the King hath commanded in the land of Iudea.

text.DRB: And the three cities that are added to Jury of the country of Samaria, let them be accounted with Jury: that they may be under one, and obey no other power, but the high priest's:
text.KJV: And concerning the three gouernments that are added to Iudea from the countrey of Samaria, let them be ioyned with Iudea, that they may be reckoned to be vnder one, nor bound to obey other authoritie then þ high priests

text.DRB: Ptolomais, and the confines thereof, which I have given for a gift to the holies, that are in Jerusalem, to the necessary charges of the holy things.
text.KJV: As for Ptolemais and the land pertaining thereto, I giue it as a free gift to the Sanctuary at Ierusalem, for the necessary expences of the Sanctuary.

text.DRB: And I give every year fifteen thousand sicles of silver of the king's accounts, that belong to me:
text.KJV: Moreouer, I giue euery yeere fifteene thousand shekels of siluer, out of the Kings accompts from the places appertaining.

text.DRB: & all that is above which they had not rendered, that were over the affairs the years before, from this time they shall give it to the works of the house.
text.KJV: And all the ouerplus which the officers payed not in as in former time, from henceforth shalbe giuen towards the workes of the Temple.

text.DRB: And beside these five thousand sicles of silver, which they received of the account of the holies every year: & let these pertain to the priests, that execute the ministry.
text.KJV: And besides this, the fiue thousand shekels of siluer, which they tooke from the vses of the Temple out of the accompts yeere by yeere, euen those things shall be released, because they appertaine to the Priests that minister.

text.DRB: And whosoever fly into the temple that is in Jerusalem, and in all the coasts thereof, in the king's danger for matter, let them be dismissed, & all things, that they have in my kingdom, let them have it free.
text.KJV: And whosoeuer they be that flee vnto the Temple at Ierusalem, or be within the liberties thereof, being indebted vnto the King, or for any other matter, let them be at libertie, and all that they haue in my realme.

text.DRB: And to build or repair the works of the holy places, the charges shall be given of the king's account:
text.KJV: For the building also and repairing of the workes of the Sanctuary, expences shalbe giuen of the Kings accompts.

text.DRB: and to build the walls in Jerusalem, and to fortify them round about, the charges shall be given of the king's account, and to build the walls in Jurie.
text.KJV: Yea, and for the building of the walles of Ierusalem, and the fortifying thereof round about, expences shall bee giuen out of the Kings accompts, as also for building of the walles in Iudea.

text.DRB: But as Jonathas, & the people heard these words, they believed them not, nor received them: because they remembered the great mischief, that he had done in Israel, and had afflicted them exceedingly.
text.KJV: Now when Ionathan and the people heard these words, they gaue no credite vnto them, nor receiued them, because they remembred the great euill that he had done in Israel; for hee had afflicted them very sore.

text.DRB: And their liking was toward Alexander, because he had been the first mover of the words of peace unto them, & him they aided all days.
text.KJV: But with Alexander they were well pleased, because hee was the first that entreated of peace with them, and they were confederate with him alwayes.

text.DRB: And king Alexander gathered a great army, & moved the camp near to Demetrius.
text.KJV: Then gathered king Alexander great forces, and camped ouer against Demetrius.

text.DRB: And the two kings joined battle, and the army of Demetrius fled, & Alexander pursued him, & urged them sore.
text.KJV: And after the two Kings had ioyned battell, Demetrius hoste fled: but Alexander followed after him, and preuailed against them.

text.DRB: And the battle grew very sore, till the sun went down: & Demetrius was slain in that day.
text.KJV: And he continued the battell very sore vntill the Sunne went downe, and that day was Demetrius slaine.

text.DRB: And Alexander sent legates to Ptolomee king of Ægypt according to these words, saying:
text.KJV: Afterward Alexander sent Embassadors to Ptoleme king of Egypt, with a message to this effect;

text.DRB: Because I am returned into my Kingdom, and am set in the seat of my fathers, & have obtained the princedom, & have destroyed Demetrius, & possessed our country,
text.KJV: Forsomuch as I am come againe to my realme, and am set in the throne of my progenitors, and haue gotten the dominion, and ouerthrowen Demetrius, and recouered our countrey,

text.DRB: and have joined battle with him, and he is destroyed himself, & his camp by us, and we sit in the seat of his kingdom:
text.KJV: (For after I had ioyned battell with him, both he, and his hoste was discomfited by vs, so that we sit in the throne of his kingdome)

text.DRB: and now let us make amity one with another: & give me thy daughter to wife, and I will be thy son in law, & I will give thee gifts, and to her dignity.
text.KJV: Now therefore let vs make a league of amitie together, and giue me now thy daughter to wife: & I will be thy son in law, and will giue both thee and her, gifts according to thy dignity.

text.DRB: And king Ptolomee answered, saying: Happy is the day wherein thou art returned to the land of thy fathers, and art set in the seat of their kingdom.
text.KJV: Then Ptoleme the king gaue answere, saying, Happy be the day wherein thou diddest returne into the land of thy fathers, and satest in the throne of their kingdome.

text.DRB: And now I will do to thee that which thou hast written: but meet me at Ptolomais, that we may see one another, and I may despouse her to thee as thou hast said.
text.KJV: And now will I doe to thee, as thou hast written: meet me therefore at Ptolemais, that wee may see one another, for I will marry my daughter to thee according to thy desire.

text.DRB: And Ptolomee went out of Ægypt, he & Cleopatra his daughter, & he came to Ptolomais in the year an hundred threescore and two.
text.KJV: So Ptolome went out of Egypt with his daughter Cleopatra, and they came vnto Ptolemais in the hundred threescore and second yeere.

text.DRB: And Alexander the king met him, & he gave him Cleopatra his daughter: and he made her marriage at Ptolomais, as kings in great glory.
text.KJV: Where king Alexander meeting him, gaue vnto him his daughter Cleopatra, and celebrated her marriage at Ptolemais with great glory, as the maner of kings is.

text.DRB: And king Alexander wrote to Jonathas, that he should come to meet him.
text.KJV: Now king Alexander had written vnto Ionathan, that hee should come and meete him.

text.DRB: And he went with glory to Ptolomais, & he met there the two kings, & he gave them much silver, & gold, & gifts: & he found grace in their sight.
text.KJV: Who thereupon went honourably to Ptolemais, where he met the two kings, and gaue them and their friends siluer and golde, and many presents, and found fauour in their sight.

text.DRB: And there assembled against him pestilent men of Israel, wicked men soliciting against him: & the king attended not to them.
text.KJV: At that time certaine pestilent fellowes of Israel, men of a wicked life, assembled themselues against him, to accuse him: but the king would not heare them.

text.DRB: And he commanded Jonathas to be changed of his garments, & that he should be clothed with purple: and so they did. And the king placed him to sit with himself.
text.KJV: Yea more then that, the king commanded to take off his garments, and clothe him in purple: and they did so.

text.DRB: And he said to his princes: Go ye out with him into the midst of the city, & proclaim, that no man solicit against him for any matter, & that no man be troublesome unto him for any cause.
text.KJV: Also he made him sit by himselfe, and said vnto his princes, Goe with him into the midst of the city, and make proclamation, that no man complaine against him of any matter, and that no man troble him for any maner of cause.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, as they that solicited saw his glory that was published, & him covered with purple, they all fled:
text.KJV: Now when his accusers sawe that he was honoured according to the proclamation, and clothed in purple, they fled all away.

text.DRB: & the king magnified him, & wrote him amongst his chief friends, & made him Duke, & partaker of the princedom.
text.KJV: So the king honoured him, and wrote him amongst his chiefe friends, and made him a duke, and partaker of his dominion.

text.DRB: And Jonathas returned into Jerusalem with peace & joy.
text.KJV: Afterward Ionathan returned to Ierusalem with peace and gladnes.

text.DRB: In the year an hundreth sixty five came Demetrius the son of Demetrius from Crete into the land of his fathers.
text.KJV: Furthermore, in the hundreth threescore and fifth yeere, came Demetrius sonne of Demetrius, out of Crete into the land of his fathers.

text.DRB: And Alexander the king heard it, & he was very sorry, & returned to Antioch.
text.KJV: Whereof when king Alexander heard tell, he was right sory, and returned into Antioch.

text.DRB: And Demetrius the king made Apollonius general, who was ruler of Coelesiria: & he gathered a great army, & came to Jamnia: & he sent to Jonathas the high priest,
text.KJV: Then Demetrius made Apollonius the gouernour of Coelosyria his general, who gathered together a great hoste, and camped in Iamnia and sent vnto Ionathan the high Priest, saying,

text.DRB: saying: Thou only resistest us, & I am brought into derision, & into reproach, because thou doest exercise power against us in the mountains.
text.KJV: Thou alone liftest vp thy selfe against vs, and I am laughed to scorne for thy sake, and reproched, and why doest thou vaunt thy power against vs in the mountaines?

text.DRB: Now therefore if thou trust in thy forces, come down to us in the plain, let us compare there one with another: because with me is the force of battles.
text.KJV: Now therefore if thou trustest in thine owne strength, come downe to vs into the plaine field, and there let vs trie the matter together, for with me is the power of the cities.

text.DRB: Ask, & learn who I am, & the rest, that aid me, who also say that your foot cannot stand before our face, because twice have thy fathers been put to flight in their own land:
text.KJV: Aske and learne who I am, and the rest that take our part, and they shal tel thee that thy foot is not able to stand before our face; for thy fathers haue bene twice put to flight in their owne land.

text.DRB: and now how wilt thou be able to abide the horsemen, & so great an army in the plain, where is no stone, nor rock, nor place to fly?
text.KJV: Wherefore now thou shalt not be able to abide the horsemen and so great a power in the plaine, where is neither stone nor flint, nor place to flee vnto.

text.DRB: But as Jonathas heard the words of Apollonius, he was moved in mind: & he chose ten thousand men, & went forth from Jerusalem, & Simon his brother met him to aid him.
text.KJV: So when Ionathan heard these words of Apollonius, he was moued in his mind, & choosing ten thousand men, he went out of Ierusalē, where Simon his brother met him for to helpe him.

text.DRB: And they moved the camp to Joppe, & it shut him out of the city: because the garrison of Apollonius was in Joppe, & he assaulted it.
text.KJV: And hee pitched his tents against Ioppe: but they of Ioppe shut him out of the citie, because Apollonius had a garison there.

text.DRB: And they that were in the city being put in great fear, opened to him, & Jonathas obtained Joppe.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan laid siege vnto it: whereupon they of the city let him in for feare: & so Ionathan wan Ioppe.

text.DRB: And Apollonius heard it, & he took three thousand horsemen, & a great army.
text.KJV: Whereof when Apollonius heard, he tooke three thousand horsemen with a great hoste of footmen, and went to Azotus as one that iourneyed, & therewithal drew him forth into the plaine, because he had a great number of horsemen, in whom he put his trust.

text.DRB: And he went to Azotus as making journey, and immediately he went forth into the plain: because he had a multitude of horsemen, and he trusted in them. And Jonathas pursued him unto Azotus, and they joined battle.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan followed after him to Azotus, where the armies ioyned battell.

text.DRB: And Apollonius left in the camp a thousand horsemen behind them secretly.
text.KJV: Now Apollonius had left a thousand horsemen in ambush.

text.DRB: And Jonathas understood that there was an embushment behind him, and they compassed his camp, and they threw darts upon the people from morning even unto evening.
text.KJV: And Ionathan knew that there was an ambushment behinde him; for they had compassed in his host, and cast darts at the people, from morning till euening.

text.DRB: But the people stood, as Jonathas had commanded: and their horses laboured.
text.KJV: But the people stood still, as Ionathan had commanded them: and so the enemies horses were tired.

text.DRB: And Simon put forth his army, and joined it against the legion: for the horsemen were wearied: and they were discomfited by him, and fled.
text.KJV: Then brought Simon forth his hoste, and set them against the footmen, (for the horsmen were spent) who were discomfited by him, and fled.

text.DRB: And they that were scattered by the plain, fled into Azotus, and entered into Bethdagon their idol house, there to save themselves.
text.KJV: The horsemen also being scattered in the field, fled to Azotus, and went into Bethdagō their idols temple for safety.

text.DRB: And Jonathas burnt Azotus, and the cities that were round about it, and he took the spoils of them, and the temple of Dagon: and all that fled into it, he burnt with fire.
text.KJV: But Ionathan set fire on Azotus, and the cities round about it, and tooke their spoiles, and the temple of Dagon, with them that were fled into it, he burnt with fire.

text.DRB: And there were that were slain by the sword, with these that were burnt, almost eight thousand men.
text.KJV: Thus there were burnt and slaine with the sword, well nigh eight thousand men.

text.DRB: And Jonathas removed the camp from thence, and brought it to Ascalon: and they went out of the city to meet him in great glory.
text.KJV: And from thence Ionathan remoued his hoste, and camped against Ascalon, where the men of the city came forth, and met him with great pompe.

text.DRB: And Jonathas returned into Jerusalem with his company, having many spoils.
text.KJV: After this, returned Ionathan and his hoste vnto Ierusalem, hauing many spoiles.

text.DRB: And it came to pass: as Alexander the king heard these words, he added yet to glorify Jonathas.
text.KJV: Now when king Alexander heard these things, he honoured Ionathan yet more,

text.DRB: And he sent him a chain of gold, as the custom is to be given to the cousins of kings. And he gave him Accaron and all the borders thereof in possession.
text.KJV: And sent him a buckle of golde, as the vse is to be giuen to such as are of the kings blood: he gaue him also Accaron with the borders thereof in possession.
And the king of Egypt gathered together a great host like the sand that lieth vpon the Sea shore, and many ships, and went about through deceit to get Alexanders kingdome, and ioyne it to his owne.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 Alliance of Ptolemy and Demetrius II. Then the king of Egypt gathered forces as numerous as the sands of the seashore, and many ships; and he sought by deceit to take Alexander’s kingdom and add it to his own.
text.NRSV: Then the king of Egypt gathered great forces, like the sand by the seashore, and many ships; and he tried to get possession of Alexander’s kingdom by trickery.
text.DRB: And the king of Ægypt gathered an army, as the sand that is about the sea shore, and many ships: and he sought to win the kingdom of Alexander by guile, and to add it to his own kingdom.

And he went out into Syria with peaceable words, & they opened to him the cities, and met him: because Alexander the king had commanded them to go forth to meet him, for that he was his father in law.
text.KJV: Whereupon he tooke his iourney into Syria in peaceable maner, so as they of the cities opened vnto him, and met him: for king Alexander had commanded them so to doe, because he was his father in law.

text.DRB: But when Ptolomee entered the cities, he put garrisons of soldiers in every city.
text.KJV: Now as Ptolomee entred into the cities, he set in euery one of them a garison of souldiers to kepe it.

text.DRB: And as he approached to Azotus, they shewed him the temple of Dagon burnt with fire, and Azotus, & the rest thereof thrown down, and the bodies cast forth, & the graves of them that were slain in the battle, which they had made near the way.
text.KJV: And when he came neere to Azotus, they shewed him the temple of Dagon that was burnt, and Azotus, and the suburbs thereof that were destroyed, and the bodies that were cast abroad, and them that he had burnt in the battell, for they had made heapes of them by the way where he should passe.

text.DRB: And they told the king that Jonathas did these things: to raise envy against him: and the king held his peace.
text.KJV: Also they told the king whatsoeuer Ionathan had done, to the intent he might blame him: but the king helde his peace.

text.DRB: And Jonathas came to meet the king into Joppe with glory, and they saluted one another, and they slept there.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan met the king with great pompe at Ioppa, where they saluted one another, and lodged.

text.DRB: And Jonathas went with the king even to the river, that was called Eleutherus, and he returned into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Afterward Ionathan when he had gone with the king to the riuer called Eleutherus, returned againe to Ierusalem.

text.DRB: And king Ptolomee obtained the dominion of the cities even to Seleucia by the sea side, and he purposed evil purposes against Alexander.
text.KJV: King Ptolomee therefore hauing gotten the dominion of the cities by the sea, vnto Seleucia vpon the sea coast, imagined wicked counsels against Alexander.

text.DRB: And sent legates to Demetrius, saying: Come, let us make a league between us, and I will give thee my daughter that Alexander hath, and thou shalt reign in the kingdom of thy father.
text.KJV: Whereupon he sent embassadours vnto king Demetrius, saying, Come, let vs make a league betwixt vs, and I will giue thee my daughter whome Alexander hath, and thou shalt reigne in thy fathers kingdome:

text.DRB: For it repenteth me that I have given him my daughter: for he hath sought to kill me.
text.KJV: For I repent þ I gaue my daughter vnto him, for he sought to slay me.

text.DRB: And he dispraised him, for that he coveted his kingdom,
text.KJV: Thus did he slander him, because he was desirous of his kingdome.

text.DRB: and he took away his daughter, & gave her to Demetrius, and alienated himself from Alexander, and his enmities were made manifest.
text.KJV: Wherefore he tooke his daughter from him, and gaue her to Demetrius, and forsooke Alexander, so that their hatred was openly knowen.
Then Ptolomee entred into Antioch, where he set two crownes vpō his head, the crowne of Asia, and of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 11:13 Then Ptolemy entered Antioch and assumed the crown of Asia; thus he set upon his head two crowns, that of Egypt and that of Asia.
text.NRSV: Ptolemy entered Antioch and put on his head the crown of Asia. Thus he put two crowns on his head, the crown of Egypt and that of Asia.
text.DRB: And Ptolomee entered into Antioch, & he put two crowns upon his head, of Ægypt, & of Asia.
But Alexander the king was in Cilicia at that time: because they rebelled that were in those places.
text.KJV: In the meane season was king Alexander in Cilicia, because those þ dwelt in those parts, had reuolted from him.

text.DRB: And Alexander heard it, and came to him into battle: and Ptolomee the king brought forth an army, and met him with a strong power, and put him to flight.
text.KJV: But when Alexander heard of this, hee came to warre against him, whereupon king Ptolomee brought forth his hoste, and met him with a mightie power, and put him to flight.
So Alexander fled into Arabia, there to be defended, but king Ptolomee was exalted.
text.NABRE: 11:16 When Alexander fled to Arabia to seek protection, King Ptolemy was triumphant.
text.NRSV: Antiochus was removed and King Ptolemy took possession of the kingdom.
text.DRB: And Alexander fled into Arabia, there to be protected: and king Ptolomee was exalted.
And Zabdiel an Arabian took off Alexander's head, & sent it to Ptolomee.
text.KJV: For Zabdiel the Arabian tooke off Alexanders head, and sent it vnto Ptolomee.

text.DRB: And king Ptolomee died the third day: & they that were in the fortresses, perished by them that were within the camp.
text.KJV: King Ptolemee also died the third day after, & they that were in the strong holds, were slaine one of another.

text.DRB: And Demetrius reigned the year an hundreth sixty seven.
text.KJV: By this meanes Demetrius reigned in the hundreth, threescore and seuenth yeere.

text.DRB: In those days Jonathas gathered together them, that were in Jurie, to win the castle in Jerusalem: and they made engines against it.
text.KJV: At the same time Ionathan gathered together them that were in Iudea, to take the towre that was in Ierusalem, and he made many engines of warre against it.

text.DRB: And certain that hated their own nation wicked men, went to king Demetrius, and told him that Jonathas besieged the castle.
text.KJV: Then certaine vngodly persons who hated their owne people, went vnto the king, and told him that Ionathan besieged the towre.

text.DRB: And as he heard it, he was wrath: and forthwith he came to Ptolemais, and wrote to Jonathas, that he should not besiege the castle, but should meet him to talk together in haste.
text.KJV: Whereof when he heard, he was angry, and immediately remouing, he can to Ptolemais, and wrote vnto Ionathan, that he should not lay siege to the towre, but come and speake with him at Ptolemais in great haste.

text.DRB: But as Jonathas heard it, he bade them besiege it: and he chose of the ancients of Israel, and of the Priests, and put himself in hazard.
text.KJV: Neuerthelesse Ionathan when he heard this, commanded to besiege it and he chose certaine of the Elders of Israel, and the priests, and put himselfe in perill.

text.DRB: And he took gold, & silver, and raiments, & many other presents, & went to the king to Ptolomais, and he found grace in his sight.
text.KJV: And tooke siluer and gold, and rayment, and diuers presents besides, and went to Ptolemais, vnto the king, where he found fauour in his sight.

text.DRB: And certain wicked men of his nation solicited against him.
text.KJV: And though certaine vngodly men of the people, had made complaints against him,

text.DRB: And the king did to him, as they had done to him which had been before him: and he exalted him in the sight of all his friends,
text.KJV: Yet the king entreated him as his predecessors had done before, & promoted him in the sight of all his friends,

text.DRB: and he established to him the chief Priesthood, & whatsoever other things he had before precious, & he made him the chief of his friends.
text.KJV: And confirmed him in the high priesthood, and in all the honours that hee had before, and gaue him preeminence among his chiefe friends.

text.DRB: And Jonathas requested of the king that he would make Jury free, & the three toparchies, and Samaria, & the confines thereof: and he promised him three hundred talents.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan desired the king, that hee would make Iudea free from tribute, as also the three gouernments with the countrey of Samaria, & he promised him three hundred talents

text.DRB: And the king consented: and he wrote to Jonathas epistles of all these things, containing this tenure.
text.KJV: So the king consented and wrote letters vnto Ionathan, of all these things after this maner.

text.DRB: King Demetrius to his brother Jonathas greeting, and to the nation of the Jews.
text.KJV: King Demetrius vnto his brother Ionathan, and vnto the nation of the Iewes, sendeth greeting.

text.DRB: Acopy of the epistle, which we have written to Lasthenes our parent concerning you, we have sent to you that you might know it.
text.KJV: We send you heere a copie of the letter, which we did write vnto our cousin Lasthenes, concerning you, that you might see it.

text.DRB: King Demetrius to Lasthenes his parent greeting.
text.KJV: King Demetrius vnto his father Lasthenes, sendeth greeting:

text.DRB: We have determined to do good to the nation of the Jews our friends, & that keep the things that are just with us, for their gentleness which they bear towards us.
text.KJV: We are determined to doe good to the people of the Iewes, who are our friends, and keepe couenants with vs, because of their good will towards vs.

text.DRB: We have ordained therefore unto them all the coasts of Jurie, & the three cities, Apherema, Lyda, & Ramatha, which are added to Jurie of Samaria, & all their confines, to be sequestered to all them that sacrifice in Jerusalem, for these things, which the king received of them every year, and for the fruits of the land, and of the trees.
text.KJV: Wherefore we haue ratified vnto them the borders of Iudea, with the three gouernments of Apherema, and Lidda, and Ramathem, that are added vnto Iudea, from the countrie of Samaria, and all things appertaining vnto them, for all such, as doe sacrifice in Ierusalem, in stead of the paiments, which the king receiued of them yeerely aforetime out of the fruits of the earth, and of trees.

text.DRB: And other things, that pertained to us of the tithes, and of the tributes, from this time we remit to them: & the salt floors, and the crowns, that were presented to us,
text.KJV: And as for other things that belong vnto vs of the tithes and customes pertaining vnto vs, as also the salt pits, and the crowne taxes, which are due vnto vs, we discharge them of them all for their reliefe.

text.DRB: we grant all to them, & nothing of these shall be broken from this time, and for ever.
text.KJV: And nothing heereof shall be reuoked from this time foorth for euer.

text.DRB: Now therefore provide to make a copy of these, and let it be given to Jonathas, & let it be laid in the holy mount, and in a solemn place.
text.KJV: Now therefore see that thou make a copie of these things, and let it be deliuered vnto Ionathan, and set vpon the holy mount in a conspicuous place.

text.DRB: And Demetrius the king seeing that the land was quiet in his sight, & nothing resisted him, he dismissed all his army, every man to his place, except the foreign army, which he brought from the isles of the Gentiles: and all the bands of his fathers were enemies to him.
text.KJV: After this, when king Demetrius saw that the land was quiet before him, and that no resistance was made against him, he sent away all his forces euery one to his owne place, except certaine bands of strangers, whom he had gathered from the iles of the heathen, wherefore all the forces of his fathers hated him.

text.DRB: And there was one Tryphon of Alexander's part before: and he saw that all the army murmured against Demetrius, & he went to Emalchuel the Arabian, that brought up Antiochus the son of Alexander:
text.KJV: Moreouer there was one Tryphon, that had beene of Alexanders part afore, who seeing that all the hoste murmured against Demetrius, went to Simalcue the Arabian, that brought vp Antiochus þ yong sonne of Alexander,

text.DRB: And he lay upon him, to deliver this Antiochus unto him, to reign in his father's place: and he declared unto him what great things Demetrius did, and the enmities of his hosts against him. And he tarried there many days.
text.KJV: And lay sore vpon him, to deliuer him that he might raigne in his fathers stead: he told him therefore all that Demetrius had done, and how his men of warre were at enmitie with him, and there he remained a long season.

text.DRB: And Jonathas sent to Demetrius the king, that he would cast out them, that were in the castle in Jerusalem, and that were in the holds: because they impugned Israel.
text.KJV: In the meane time Ionathan sent vnto king Demetrius, that hee would cast those of the towre out of Ierusalem, and those also in the fortresses. For they fought against Israel.

text.DRB: And Demetrius sent to Jonathas, saying: I will not only do these things for thee, & for thy nation, but with glory I will honour thee, & thy nation, when it shall be time convenient.
text.KJV: So Demetrius sent vnto Ionathan, saying, I will not onely doe this for thee, and thy people, but I will greatly honour thee and thy nation, if opportunitie serue.

text.DRB: Now therefore thou shalt do well if thou wilt send men to help me: because all mine army is departed.
text.KJV: Now therefore thou shalt do wel if thou send me men to helpe me; for all my forces are gone from me.

text.DRB: And Jonathas sent him three thousand valiant men to Antioch: and they came to the king, & the king was delighted at their coming.
text.KJV: Upon this Ionathan sent him three thousand strong men vnto Antioch, and when they came to þ king, the king was very glad of their comming.

text.DRB: And there assembled they that were of the city, an hundred twenty thousand men, and they would have slain the king.
text.KJV: Howbeit, they that were of the citie, gathered themselues together into the midst of the citie, to the number of an hundreth and twentie thousand men, and would haue slaine the king.

text.DRB: And the king fled into the court: & they that were of the city kept the ways of the city, and began to fight.
text.KJV: Wherefore the king fled into the court, but they of the citie kept the passages of the citie, and began to fight.

text.DRB: And the king called the Jews for aid: & they came together to him all at once, & they were all dispersed through the city.
text.KJV: Then the king called to the Iewes for helpe, who came vnto him all at once, and dispersing themselues through the city, slew that day in the citie to the number of an hundred thousand.

text.DRB: And they slew in that day an hundred thousand men, & they burnt the city, & took many spoils in that day, & delivered the king.
text.KJV: Also they set fire on the citie, and gat many spoiles that day, and deliuered the king.

text.DRB: And they that were of the city saw, that the Jews had obtained the city as they would: and they were discomfited in their mind, and cried to the king with petitions, saying:
text.KJV: So when they of the city saw, that the Iewes had got the city as they would, their courage was abated, wherefore they made supplication to the king, and cried, saying:

text.DRB: Give us the right hand, and let the Jews cease to oppugn us & the city.
text.KJV: Graunt vs peace, and let the Iewes cease from assaulting vs and the citie.

text.DRB: And they threw away their weapons, & made peace, & the Jews were glorified in the king's sight, & were renowned in the sight of all that were in his kingdom, & returned into Jerusalem with many spoils.
text.KJV: With that they cast away their weapons, and made peace, and the Iewes were honoured in the sight of the king, and in the sight of all that were in his realme, and they returned to Ierusalem hauing great spoiles.

text.DRB: And king Demetrius sat in the seat of his kingdom: and the land was at rest in his sight.
text.KJV: So king Demetrius sate on the throne of his kingdome, and the land was quiet before him.

text.DRB: And he feigned all things whatsoever he said, & alienated himself from Jonathas, and he requited him not according to the benefits, that he had given him, and he vexed him exceedingly.
text.KJV: Neuerthelesse hee dissembled in all that euer hee spake, and estranged himselfe from Ionathan, neither rewarded he him, according to the benefits which hee had receiued of him, but troubled him very sore.

text.DRB: And after these things Tryphon returned, & with him Antiochus the young boy, who reigned, and put the crown upon him.
text.KJV: After this returned Tryphon, and with him the yong childe Antiochus, who reigned and was crowned.

text.DRB: And there gathered unto him all the bands, which Demetrius had dispersed, and they fought against him: & he fled, and turned the back.
text.KJV: Then there gathered vnto him all the men of warre whom Demetrius had put away, and they fought against Demetrius, who turned his backe and fled.

text.DRB: And Tryphon took the beasts, and won Antioch.
text.KJV: Moreouer Triphon tooke the Elephants, and wonne Antioch.
At that time yong Antiochus wrote vnto Ionathan, saying; I confirme thee in the high Priesthood, and appoint thee ruler ouer the foure gouernments, and to be one of the kings friends.
text.NABRE: 11:57 Then young Antiochus wrote to Jonathan: “I confirm you in the high priesthood and appoint you ruler over the four districts, and to be one of the King’s Friends.”
text.NRSV: Young Antiochus wrote to Jonathan, saying: “I confirm you in the high priesthood and set you over the four districts and appoint you to be one of the king’s friends.”
text.DRB: And Antiochus the young man wrote to Jonathas, saying: I appoint to thee the priesthood, & I place thee over the four cities, that thou mayst be of the king's friends.
And he sent him vessels of gold for his service, and he gave him leave to drink in gold, and to be in purple, & to have a chain of gold:
text.KJV: Upon this he sent him golden vessels to be serued in, and gaue him leaue to drinke in gold, and to bee clothed in purple, and to weare a golden buckle.

text.DRB: And he appointed Simon his brother governor from the borders of Tyre even to the coasts of Ægypt.
text.KJV: His brother Simon also he made captaine from the place called the ladder of Tyrus, vnto the borders of Egypt.

text.DRB: And Jonathas went forth, & walked through the cities beyond the river: and all the band of Syria gathered unto him to help him, and he came to Ascalon, & they met him honourably out of the city.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan went foorth and passed through the cities beyond the water, and all the forces of Syria, gathered themselues vnto him for to helpe him: and when he came to Ascalon, they of the city met him honorably.

text.DRB: And he went from thence to Gaza: and they that were in Gaza shut in themselves: and he besieged it, & burnt the things that were round about the city, and he spoiled it.
text.KJV: From whence he went to Gaza, but they of Gaza shut him out; wherefore hee layd siege vnto it, and burned the suburbs thereof with fire, and spoiled them.

text.DRB: And the men of Gaza besought Jonathas, and he gave them the right hand: and he took their sons for hostages, & sent them into Jerusalem: and he walked through the country even to Damascus.
text.KJV: Afterward when they of Gaza made supplication vnto Ionathan, he made peace with them, and tooke the sonnes of the chiefe men for hostages, and sent them to Ierusalem, and passed through the countrey vnto Damascus.

text.DRB: And Jonathas heard that the princes of Demetrius prevaricated in Cades, which is in Galilee, with a great army meaning to remove him from the affairs of the kingdom:
text.KJV: Now when Ionathan heard that Demetrius Princes were come to Cades which is in Galilee, with a great power, purposing to remoue him out of the countrey,

text.DRB: and he went against them: but his brother Simon he left within the Province.
text.KJV: Hee went to meet them, and left Simon his brother in the countrey.

text.DRB: And Simon approached to Bethsura, and expugned it many days, and inclosed them.
text.KJV: Then Simon encamped against Bethsura, and fought against it a long season, and shut it vp:

text.DRB: And they requested of him to take the right hands, and he gave it them: and he cast them out from thence, and took the city, & placed therein a garrison.
text.KJV: But they desired to haue peace with him, which he granted them, and then put them out from thence, and tooke the city, and set a garrison in it.

text.DRB: And Jonathas and his camp approached to the water of Genesar, and before it was light they watched in the plain Asor.
text.KJV: As for Ionathan and his hoste, they pitched at the water of Gennesar, from whence betimes in the morning they gate them to the plaine of Nasor.

text.DRB: And lo the camp of the aliens met him in the plain, and they laid embushments for him in the mountains: but he went against them.
text.KJV: And behold, the hoste of strangers met them in the plaine, who hauing layed men in ambush for him in the mountaines, came themselues ouer against him.

text.DRB: And the embushments rose out of their places, & they joined battle.
text.KJV: So when they that lay in ambush rose out of their places, and ioyned battel, al that were of Ionathans side fled.

text.DRB: And all fled that were on Jonathas' part, and none was left of them, but Mathathias the son of Absolom, and Judas the son of Calphi, chief captain of the chivalry of the host.
text.KJV: In so much as there was not one of them left, except Mattathias the sonne of Absolon, and Iudas the sonne of Calphi the captaines of the hoste.

text.DRB: And Jonathas rent his garments, and put earth upon his head, and prayed.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan rent his clothes, and cast earth vpon his head, and prayed.

text.DRB: And Jonathas returned to them into battle, and he put them to flight, and they fought.
text.KJV: Afterwards turning againe to battell, he put them to flight, and so they ranne away.

text.DRB: And they of his part that fled saw, and returned to him, and pursued with him even to Cades to their camp, and they came even thither.
text.KJV: Now when his owne men that were fled saw this, they turned againe vnto him, and with him pursued them to Cades, euen vnto their owne tents, and there they camped.

text.DRB: And there fell of the aliens in that day three thousand men: and Jonathas returned into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: So there were slaine of the heathen that day, about three thousand men, but Ionathan returned to Ierusalem.
Nowe when Ionathan saw that the time serued him, he chose certaine men and sent them to Rome, for to confirme and renew the friendship that they had with them.
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 Alliances with Rome and Sparta. When Jonathan saw that the time was right, he chose men and sent them to Rome to confirm and renew the friendship with the Romans.
text.NRSV: Now when Jonathan saw that the time was favorable for him, he chose men and sent them to Rome to confirm and renew the friendship with them.
text.DRB: And Jonathas saw that the time helped him, he chose men & sent them to Rome, to establish and to renew amity with them:
and to the Spartiates, & to other places he sent epistles according to the same tenure.
text.KJV: He sent letters also to the Lacedemonians, and to other places, for the same purpose.

text.DRB: And they went to Rome, and entered into the court, and said: Jonathas the high priest, and the nation of the Jews have sent us to renew the amity and society according as before.
text.KJV: So they went vnto Rome, and entred into the Senate, and said, Ionathan the high Priest, and the people of the Iewes sent vs vnto you, to the end you should renew the friendship which you had with them, and league, as in former time.

text.DRB: And they gave them epistles unto them by place and place, to conduct them into the land of Juda with peace.
text.KJV: Upon this the Romanes gaue them letters vnto the gouernours of euery place, that they should bring them into the land of Iudea peaceably.

text.DRB: And this is a copy of the epistles which Jonathas wrote to the Spartiates:
text.KJV: And this is the copy of the letters which Ionathan wrote to the Lacedemonians:

text.DRB: Jonathas the high Priest, and the ancients of the nation, and the Priests, and the rest of the people of the Jews, to the Spartiates their brethren greeting.
text.KJV: Ionathan the hie Priest, and the Elders of the nation, and the Priestes and the other people of the Iewes, vnto the Lacedemonians their brethren, send greeting.

text.DRB: Long ago were epistles sent to Onias the high-Priest from Arius who reigned with you, that you are our brethren, as the writing containeth, that here beneath followeth.
text.KJV: There were letters sent in times past vnto Onias the high Priest from Darius, who reigned then among you, to signifie that you are our brethren, as the copy here vnder-written doeth specifie.

text.DRB: And Onias received the man that was sent with honour: and he received the epistles, wherein there was signification of the society and amity.
text.KJV: At which time Onias intreated the Embassador that was sent, honourably, and receiued the letters, wherein declaration was made of the league and friendship.

text.DRB: We whereas we needed none of these, having for our comfort the holy books, that are in our hands,
text.KJV: Therefore we also, albeit we need none of these things, for that wee haue the holy bookes of Scripture in our hands to comfort vs,

text.DRB: chose rather to send unto you to renew the fraternity, lest perhaps we be made aliens from you: for much time is passed, since you sent to us.
text.KJV: Haue neuerthelesse attempted to send vnto you, for the renewing of brotherhood and friendship, lest we should become strangers vnto you altogether: for there is a long time passed since you sent vnto vs.

text.DRB: We therefore at all time without intermission in the solemn days, and the rest wherein we should, are mindful of you in the sacrifices, that we offer, and in the observances, as it is meet, and becometh to remember brethren.
text.KJV: We therefore at all times without ceasing, both in our Feasts, and other conuenient dayes, doe remember you in the sacrifices which we offer, and in our prayers, as reason is, and as it becommeth vs to thinke vpon our brethren:

text.DRB: We rejoice therefore of your glory.
text.KJV: And wee are right glad of your honour.

text.DRB: But many tribulations have compassed us, and many battles, and the kings that are round about us, have impugned us.
text.KJV: As for our selues, wee haue had great troubles and warres on euery side, forsomuch as the kings that are round about vs haue fought against vs.

text.DRB: We therefore would not be troublesome to you, nor to the rest our fellows & friends in these battles.
text.KJV: Howbeit wee would not be troublesome vnto you, nor to others of our confederates & friends in these warres:

text.DRB: For we have had aid from heaven, and we are delivered, and our enemies are humbled.
text.KJV: For wee haue helpe from heauen that succoureth vs, so as we are deliuered from our enemies, and our enemies are brought vnder foote.

text.DRB: We have chosen therefore Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the son of Jason, and have sent to the Romans to renew with them the old amity and society.
text.KJV: For this cause we chose Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the sonne of Iason, and sent them vnto the Romanes, to renew the amitie that we had with them, and the former league.

text.DRB: We therefore have commanded them that they come unto you also, and salute you, and deliver you our epistles, concerning the renewing of our fraternity.
text.KJV: We commanded them also to goe vnto you, and to salute you, and to deliuer you our letters, concerning the renewing of our brotherhood.

text.DRB: And now you shall do well in answering us to these things.
text.KJV: Wherefore now ye shall doe well to giue vs an answere thereto.

text.DRB: And this is the rescript of the epistles, which he had sent to Onias:
text.KJV: And this is the copy of the letters which Omiares sent:

text.DRB: Arius the king of the Spartians to Onias the grand Priest greeting.
text.KJV: Areus king of the Lacedemonians, to Onias the hie Priest, greeting.

text.DRB: It is found in scripture of the Spartians, and the Jews, that they are brethren, & that they are of the stock of Abraham.
text.KJV: It is found in writing, that the Lacedemonians and Iewes are brethren, and that they are of the stocke of Abraham:

text.DRB: And now since we understood these things, you do well in writing to us of your peace.
text.KJV: Now therefore, since this is come to our knowledge, you shall doe well to write vnto vs of your prosperitie.

text.DRB: Yea and we have written again to you, Our cattle, and our possessions, are yours: and yours, ours. We therefore have commanded that these things be told you.
text.KJV: We doe write backe againe to you, that your cattell and goods are ours, and ours are yours. We doe command therefore to make report vnto you on this wise.

text.DRB: And Jonathas heard that the princes of Demetrius were come again with a great army more than before to fight against him.
text.KJV: Now when Ionathan heard that Demetrius princes were come to fight against him with a greater hoste then afore,

text.DRB: And he went out from Jerusalem, & met them in the country of Amathis: for he gave them not space to enter into his country.
text.KJV: Hee remooued from Ierusalem, and met them in the land of Amathis: for he gaue them no respite to enter his countrey.

text.DRB: And he sent spies into their camp, and they returning reported that they appointed to come upon them in the night.
text.KJV: He sent spies also vnto their tents, who came againe, and tolde him, that they were appointed to come vpon them in the night season.

text.DRB: And when the sun was set, Jonathas commanded his men to watch, and to be in armour ready to fight all night, and he set watchmen round about the camp.
text.KJV: Wherefore so soone as the Sunne was downe, Ionathan commaunded his men to watch, and to be in armes, that all the night long they might bee ready to fight: Also he sent foorth sentinels round about the hoste.

text.DRB: And the adversaries heard that Jonathas with his company was prepared to battle: & they feared & were afraid in their heart: and they kindled fires in their camp.
text.KJV: But when the aduersaries heard that Ionathan and his men were ready for battell, they feared, and trembled in their hearts, and they kindled fires in their campe.

text.DRB: But Jonathas, & they that were with him, knew it not till the morning, and they saw light burning,
text.KJV: Howbeit Ionathan and his company knew it not till the morning: for they saw the lights burning.

text.DRB: and Jonathas followed them, & overtook them not: for they had passed the river Eleutherus.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan pursued after them, but ouertooke them not: for they were gone ouer the riuer Eleutherus.

text.DRB: And Jonathas turned aside to the Arabians, that were called Zabadeans, & he struck them, & took the spoils of them.
text.KJV: Wherefore Ionathan turned to the Arabians, who were called Zabadeans, and smote them, and tooke their spoiles.

text.DRB: And he joined, & came to Damascus, & walked through all that country.
text.KJV: And remouing thence, he came to Damascus, and so passed through all the countrey.

text.DRB: But Simon went forth, and came even to Ascalon, and to the next holds, and he declined into Joppe, and took it.
text.KJV: Simon also went foorth, and passed through the countrey vnto Ascalon, and the holds there adioyning, from whence he turned aside to Ioppe, and wanne it.

text.DRB: (For he heard that they would deliver the hold to Demetrius' part) and he put a garrison there to keep it.
text.KJV: For he had heard that they would deliuer the hold vnto them that tooke Demetrius part, wherefore he set a garison there to keepe it.

text.DRB: And Jonathas returned, and called together the ancients of the people, & he consulted with them to build fortresses in Jurie,
text.KJV: After this came Ionathan home againe, and calling the Elders of the people together, hee consulted with them about building strong holdes in Iudea,

text.DRB: & to build the walls in Jerusalem, and to rear a mount of a great height between the castle and the city, to separate it from the city, that it might be alone, and they might neither buy nor sell:
text.KJV: And making the walles of Ierusalem higher, and raising a great mount betweene the towre and the city, for to separate it from the city, that so it might be alone, that men might neither sell nor buy in it.

text.DRB: And they came together to build the city: and the wall fell that was upon the torrent toward the rising of the sun, and he repaired it, which is called Caphetetha:
text.KJV: Upon this they came together, to build vp the citie forasmuch as the wall toward the brooke on the East side was fallen down, & they repaired that which was called Caphenatha

text.DRB: and Simon built Adiada in Sephela, and fortified it, and hanged on gates & locks.
text.KJV: Simon also set vp Adida, in Sephela, and made it strong with gates and barres.

text.DRB: And when Tryphon had purposed to reign over Asia, and to take the crown, and to extend his hand against Antiochus the king:
text.KJV: Now Tryphon went about to get the kingdome of Asia, and to kill Antiochus the king, that hee might set the crowne vpon his owne head.

text.DRB: fearing lest perhaps Jonathas would not permit him, but would fight against him, he sought to take him and to kill him. And rising up he went into Bethsan.
text.KJV: Howbeit, he was afraid that Ionathan would not suffer him, and that he would fight against him, wherefore he sought a way, howe to take Ionathan, that he might kill him. So he remoued, and came to Bethsan.

text.DRB: And Jonathas went forth to meet him with forty thousand chosen men into battle, and came to Bethsan.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan went out to meet him with fourtie thousand men, chosen for the battell, and came to Bethsan.

text.DRB: And Tryphon saw that Jonathas came with a great army, to extend his hands upon him: and he feared:
text.KJV: Now when Tryphon saw that Ionathan came with so great a force, hee durst not stretch his hande against him,

text.DRB: and received him with honour, & commanded him to all his friends, and gave him gifts: and he commanded his hosts to obey him, as himself.
text.KJV: But receiued him honourably, and cōmended him vnto all his friends, and gaue him gifts, and commaunded his men of warre to be as obedient vnto him, as to himselfe.

text.DRB: And he said to Jonathas: Why hast thou troubled all the people, whereas we have no war?
text.KJV: Unto Ionathan also hee said, Why hast thou put all this people to so great trouble, seeing there is no warre betwixt vs?

text.DRB: And now send them back to their houses, but choose thee a few men, that may be with thee, and come with me to Ptolomais, and I will deliver it to thee, and the other holds, and the army, and all the overseers of the affairs, and returning I will depart: for therefore I came.
text.KJV: Therefore send them now home againe, and chuse a few men to waite on thee, and come thou with me to Ptolemais, for I will giue it thee and the rest of the strong holds and forces, and all that haue any charge: as for me, I will returne and depart: for this is the cause of my comming.

text.DRB: And Jonathas believed him, and did as he said: and dismissed his army, and they departed into the land of Juda:
text.KJV: So Ionathan beleeuing him, did as he bade him, and sent away his host, who went into the land of Iudea.

text.DRB: but he retained with him three thousand men: of whom he sent back two thousand into Galilee, and a thousand came with him.
text.KJV: And with himselfe hee retained but three thousand men, of whome he sent two thousand into Galile, and one thousand went with him.
Now assoone as Ionathan entred into Ptolemais, they of Ptolemais shut the gates, and tooke him, and all them that came with him, they shewe with the sword.
text.NABRE: 12:48 Then as soon as Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him; all who had entered with him, they killed with the sword.
text.NRSV: But as soon as Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him, and all who had entered with him they killed with the sword.
text.DRB: But as Jonathas entered into Ptolemais, they of Ptolemais shut the gates of the city, and they took him, and all that had entered with him they slew with the sword.
And Tryphon sent an army and horsemen into Galilee, and into the great plain to destroy all Jonathas' fellows.
text.KJV: Then sent Tryphon an hoste of footmen, and horsemen into Galile, and into the great plaine, to destroy all Ionathans company.

text.DRB: But they when they understood that Jonathas was taken, & was perished, and all that were with him, they encouraged themselves, and went forth ready to battle.
text.KJV: But when they knew that Ionathan and they that were with him were taken and slaine, they encouraged one another, and went close together, prepared to fight.

text.DRB: And these that had pursued, seeing that the matter stood upon their life, they returned.
text.KJV: They therfore that followed vpon them, perceiuing þ they were ready to fight for their liues, turned back againe.

text.DRB: But they came all with peace into the land of Juda. And they lamented Jonathas, & them that were with him exceedingly: and Israel mourned with great mourning.
text.KJV: Whereupon they all came into the land of Iudea peaceably, and there they bewailed Ionathan & them that were with him, & they were sore afraid, wherfore all Israel made great lamentation.

text.DRB: And all the heathen that were round about them, sought to destroy them, for they said:
text.KJV: Then all the heathen that were round about them, sought to destroy them. For, said they, they haue no captaine, nor any to helpe them. Now therfore let vs make war vpon them, & take away their memorial frō amongst men.

text.DRB: They have no prince, and helper: now therefore let us expugn them, and take away from men the memory of them.

text.KJV: Now when Simon heard that Tryphon had gathered together a great hoste to inuade the land of Iudea, and destroy it,
text.NABRE: 13:1 IV. Leadership of Simon Chapter 13 Simon as Leader. When Simon heard that Trypho was gathering a large army to invade and ravage the land of Judah,
text.NRSV: Simon heard that Trypho had gathered a large army to invade the land of Judah and destroy it.
text.DRB: And Simon heard that Tryphon gathered a very great army, to come into the land of Juda, and to destroy it.
Seeing that the people was in dread, and in fear, he went up to Jerusalem, and assembled the people:
text.KJV: And saw that the people was in great trembling and feare, he went vp to Ierusalem, and gathered the people together,

text.DRB: and exhorting he said: You know what great battles I and my brethren, and the house of my father have made for the laws, and the holies, and the distresses that we have seen:
text.KJV: And gaue them exhortation, saying: Yee your selues know, what great things I and my brethren, and my fathers house haue done for the lawes, and the Sanctuarie, the battels also, and troubles which we haue seene,

text.DRB: by reason of these are all my brethren perished for Israel, and I only am left.
text.KJV: By reason whereof all my brethren are slaine for Israels sake, and I am left alone.

text.DRB: And now let it not fall to me to spare my life in all time of tribulation: for I am not better than my brethren.
text.KJV: Now therefore be it farre from me, that I should spare mine owne life in any time of trouble: for I am no better then my brethren.
