text.DRB: I will avenge therefore my nation and the holies, our children also, and wives: because all the heathen are gathered together to destroy us of enmity.
text.KJV: Doubtlesse I will auenge my nation and the Sanctuarie, & our wiues, and our children: for all the heathen are gathered to destroy vs, of very malice.

text.DRB: And the spirit of the people was kindled as soon as they heard these words:
text.KJV: Now as soone as the people heard these words, their spirit reuiued.
And they answered with a loud voice, saying, Thou shalt bee our leader in stead of Iudas and Ionathan thy brother.
text.NABRE: 13:8 They shouted in reply: “You are our leader in place of your brothers Judas and Jonathan.
text.NRSV: The people answered with a loud voice: “You are our leader in place of Judas and Jonathan your brother.”
text.DRB: and they answered with a loud voice, saying: Thou art our captain in the place of Judas, and Jonathas thy brother:
fight thou our battle, and we will do all things whatsoever thou shalt say to us.
text.KJV: Fight thou our battels, & what soeuer thou commandest vs, that will we doe.

text.DRB: And gathering together all the men of war, he hastened to finish all the walls of Jerusalem, and fortified it round about.
text.KJV: So then he gathered together all the men of warre, and made hast to finish the walles of Ierusalem, and he fortified it round about.

text.DRB: And he sent Jonathas the son of Absalom, and with him a new army into Joppe, and they that were in the castle being cast out, he remained there.
text.KJV: Also he sent Ionathan, the sonne of Absolom, & with him a great power to Ioppe, who casting out them that were therein, remained there in it.

text.DRB: And Tryphon removed from Ptolemais with a great army, to come into the land of Juda, and Jonathas with him in ward.
text.KJV: So Tryphon remoued from Ptolemais, with a great power to inuade the land of Iudea, and Ionathan was with him in warde.

text.DRB: But Simon pitched in Addus against the face of the plain.
text.KJV: But Simon pitched his tents at Adida, ouer against the plaine.

text.DRB: And as Tryphon understood that Simon was risen in the place of his brother Jonathas: and that he would join battle with him, he sent legates to him,
text.KJV: Now when Tryphon knew that Simon, was risen vp in stead of his brother Ionathan, and meant to ioyne battell with him, he sent messengers vnto him, saying,

text.DRB: saying: For the silver, that thy brother Jonathas owed in the king's account, for the affairs which he had, we have stayed him.
text.KJV: Whereas we haue Ionathan thy brother in hold, it is for money that he is owing vnto the kings treasure, concerning the businesse that was committed vnto him.

text.DRB: And now send an hundred talents of silver, and his two sons for hostages, that being dismissed he fly not from us, and we will release him.
text.KJV: Wherefore, now send an hundred talents of siluer, and two of his sonnes for hostages, that when he is at liberty he may not reuolt from vs, and we will let him goe.

text.DRB: And Simon knew that with guile he spake with him, nevertheless he commanded the silver, and the children to be given: lest he should get great enmity with the people of Israel, saying:
text.KJV: Heereupon Simon, albeit he perceiued that they spake deceiptfully vnto him, yet sent he the money, and the children, lest peraduenture he should procure to himselfe great hatred of the people:

text.DRB: Because he sent not the silver, and the children, therefore is he perished.
text.KJV: Who might haue said, Because I sent him not the money, and the children, therefore is dead.

text.DRB: And he sent the children, and the hundred talents: and he lied, and dismissed not Jonathas.
text.KJV: So he sent them the children, and the hundred talents: Howbeit dissembled, neither would he let Ionathan goe.

text.DRB: And after these things came Tryphon within the country, to destroy it, and they compassed by the way that leadeth to Addar, and Simon, & his camp marched into every place whither soever they went.
text.KJV: And after this came Tryphon to inuade the land, and destroy it, going round about by the way that leadeth vnto Adora, but Simon and his host marched against him in euery place wheresoeuer he went.

text.DRB: And they that were in the castle sent legates to Tryphon, that he should hasten to come through the desert, and should send them victuals.
text.KJV: Now they that were in the towre, sent messengers vnto Tryphon, to the end that he should hasten his comming vnto them by the wildernesse, and send them victuals.

text.DRB: And Tryphon prepared all the horsemen to come that night: but there was an exceeding great snow, and he came not into the country of Galaad.
text.KJV: Wherefore Tryphon made readie all his horsemen to come that night, but there fell a very great snow, by reason whereof he came not: So he departed & came into the countrey of Galaad.
And when he came neere to Bascama, he slew Ionathan, who was buried there.
text.NABRE: 13:23 When he was approaching Baskama, he had Jonathan killed and buried him there.
text.NRSV: Trypho put Jonathan to death and buried him there.
text.DRB: And when he had approached to Bascama, he slew Jonathas and his sons there.
And Tryphon returned, and went into his country.
text.KJV: Afterward Tryphon returned, and went into his owne land.

text.DRB: And Simon sent and took the bones of Jonathas his brother, and buried them in Modin the city of his fathers.
text.KJV: Then sent Simon and tooke the bones of Ionathan his brother, and buried them in Modin the citie of his fathers.

text.DRB: And all Israel lamented him with great lamentation: and they mourned for him many days.
text.KJV: And all Israel made great lamentation for him, and bewailed him many daies.

text.DRB: And Simon built over the sepulchre of his father and of his brethren a building high to behold, of stone polished behind and before:
text.KJV: Simon also built a monument vpon the Sepulchre of his father and his brethren, and raised it aloft to the sight, with hewen stone behind and before.

text.DRB: and he erected seven spires one against one, to his father and mother, and to his four brethren:
text.KJV: Moreouer hee set vp seuen pyramides one against another, for his father and his mother, and his foure brethren.

text.DRB: and about these he set great pillars: and upon the pillars armour for an eternal memory: and by the armour ships graven, which might be seen of all that sailed the sea.
text.KJV: And in these he made running deuices, about the which he set great pillars, and vpon the pillars he made all their armour for a perpetuall memory, and by the armour, ships carued, that they might be seene of all that saile on the sea.

text.DRB: This is the sepulchre, that he made in Modin even until this day.
text.KJV: This is the Sepulchre which he made at Modin, and it standeth yet vnto this day.

text.DRB: But Tryphon when he journeyed with Antiochus the king, being but a young man, by guile he slew him.
text.KJV: Now Tryphon dealt deceitfully with the yong king Antiochus, and slew him,

text.DRB: And he reigned in his place, & put the crown of Asia upon himself, and made a great plague in the land.
text.KJV: And he raigned in his stead, and crowned himselfe king of Asia, and brought a great calamitie vpō the land.

text.DRB: And Simon built the fortresses of Jurie, fortifying them with high towers, & great walls, and gates, and locks: and he put victuals in the fortresses.
text.KJV: Then Simon built vp the strong holds in Iudea, and fensed them about with high towres, and great walles and gates and barres, and layd vp victuals therein.

text.DRB: And Simon chose men & sent to Demetrius the king, that he would make a release to the country: because all Tryphon's acts had been done by spoil.
text.KJV: Moreouer Simon chose men, and sent to king Demetrius, to the end he should giue the land an immunitie, because all that Tryphon did, was to spoyle.

text.DRB: And Demetrius the king according to these words answered him, and wrote an epistle in this manner:
text.KJV: Unto whom king Demetrius answered and wrote after this maner.

text.DRB: King Demetrius to Simon the High-Priest, and the friend of kings, and to the ancients, and to the nation of the Jews, greeting.
text.KJV: King Demetrius vnto Simon the high Priest, and friend of kings, as also vnto the Elders and nation of the Iewes, sendeth greeting.

text.DRB: The crown of gold, and the bahem, which you sent, we have received: and we are ready to make with you great peace, and to write to the king's chief officers to release you the things that we have released.
text.KJV: The golden crowne, and the scarlet robe which ye sent vnto vs, we haue receiued, and wee are ready to make a stedfast peace with you, yea and to write vnto our officers to confirme the immunities which we haue granted.

text.DRB: For whatsoever things we have decreed unto you, stand in force. The holds that you have built, let them be to you.
text.KJV: And whatsoeuer couenants we haue made with you, shall stand, and the strong holdes which yee haue builded shalbe your owne.

text.DRB: We remit also the ignorances, and offences even until this day, and the crown which you owed: and if any other thing was tributary in Jerusalem, now let it not be tributary.
text.KJV: As for any ouersight or fault committed vnto this day, we forgiue it, and the crowne taxe also which yee owe vs, if there were any other tribute paide in Ierusalem, it shall no more be paide.

text.DRB: And if any of you be fit to be enrolled among ours, let them be enrolled and let there be peace between us.
text.KJV: And looke who are meet among you to be in our court, let them be inrolled, and let there be peace betwixt vs.
Thus the yoke of the heathen was taken away from Israel, in the hundred and seuentieth yeere.
text.NABRE: 13:41 Thus in the one hundred and seventieth year, the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,
text.NRSV: In the one hundred seventieth year the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,
text.DRB: In the year an hundreth seventy was the yoke of the Gentiles taken away from Israel.

text.KJV: Then the people of Israel began to write in their instruments, and contracts, in the first yeere of Simon the high Priest, the gouernour, and leader of the Iewes.
text.NABRE: 13:42 and the people began to write in their records and contracts, “In the first year of Simon, great high priest, governor, and leader of the Jews.”
text.NRSV: and the people began to write in their documents and contracts, “In the first year of Simon the great high priest and commander and leader of the Jews.”
text.DRB: And the people of Israel began to write in the tables, and public instruments. The first year under Simon the High-Priest, the great Duke, and the Prince of the Jews:
In these days Simon approached to Gaza, and compassed it with camps, and he made engines, and set them to the city, and he struck one tower, and took it.
text.KJV: In those dayes Simon camped against Gaza, and besieged it round about; he made also an engine of warre, and set it by the city, and battered a certaine towre, and tooke it.

text.DRB: And they that were within the engine, brake out into the city: and there was made a great stir in the city.
text.KJV: And they that were in the Engine leapt into the citie, whereupon there was a great vproare in the citie:

text.DRB: And they that were in the city went up with their wives and children upon the wall their coats rent, and they cried with a loud voice, desiring of Simon that the right hands might be given them,
text.KJV: Insomuch as the people of the citie rent their clothes, and climed vpon the walles, with their wiues and children, and cried with a lowd voice, beseeching Simon to grant them peace.

text.DRB: and they said: Requite us not according to our malices, but according to thy mercies.
text.KJV: And they said, Deale not with vs according to our wickednesse, but according to thy mercy.

text.DRB: And Simon yielding destroyed them not: but yet he cast them out of the city, and cleansed the houses, wherein there had been idols, and then he entered into it with hymns blessing our Lord:
text.KJV: So Simon was appeased towards them, and fought no more against them, but put them out of the citie, and cleansed the houses wherein the idols were: and so entred into it, with songs, and thankesgiuing.

text.DRB: and all filthiness being cast out of it, he placed in it men that should do the law: and he fortified it, and made it his habitation.
text.KJV: Yea, he put all vncleannesse out of it, and placed such men there, as would keepe the Law, and made it stronger then it was before, and built therein a dwelling place for himselfe.

text.DRB: But they that were in the castle of Jerusalem, were prohibited to go out and to come into the country, and to buy and sell: and they were exceeding hungry, and many of them perished for famine,
text.KJV: They also of the towre in Ierusalem were kept so strait, that they could neither come foorth, nor goe into the countrey, nor buy, nor sell, wherefore they were in great distresse for want of victuals, and a great number of them perished through famine.

text.DRB: and they cried to Simon to receive the right hands, and he gave it them: and he cast them out from thence, and cleansed the castle from the contaminations:
text.KJV: Then cried they to Simon, beseeching him to bee at one with them, which thing hee graunted them, and when he had put them out from thence, he cleansed the towre from pollutions:

text.DRB: and they entered into it the three and twentieth day of the second month, the year an hundreth seventy one, with praise, and boughs of palm trees, and harps, and cymbals, and nables, and hymns, and canticles, because the great enemy was destroyed out of Israel.
text.KJV: And entred into it the three and twentieth day of the second moneth, in the hundred seuentie and one yere, with thankesgiuing, and branches of palme trees, and with harpes, and cymbals, and with viols and hymnes, and songs: because there was destroyed a great enemy out of Israel.

text.DRB: And he ordained that every year these days should be kept with gladness.
text.KJV: Hee ordained also that that day should be kept euery yeere with gladnes. Moreouer, the hill of the Temple that was by the towre he made stronger then it was, and there hee dwelt himselfe with his company.

text.DRB: And he fortified the mount of the temple, that was by the castle, and he dwelt there himself, & they that were with him.
text.KJV: And when Simon sawe that Iohn his sonne was a valiant man, he made him captaine of all the hostes and dwelt in Gazara.

text.DRB: And Simon saw John his son, that he was a valiant man of war: and he made him captain of all the hosts: and he dwelt in Gazara.
In the year an hundreth seventy two, king Demetrius gathered his army, and went into Media to get him aids to expugn Tryphon.
text.KJV: Now in the hundred threescore and twelfth yeere, king Demetrius gathered his forces together, and went into Media, to get him helpe to fight against Tryphon.

text.DRB: And Arsaces the king of Persia, and Media heard, that Demetrius was entered his confines, and he sent one of his princes to take him alive.
text.KJV: But when Arsaces the king of Persia & Media, heard that Demetrius was entred within his borders, he sent one of his princes to take him aliue.

text.DRB: And he went, and struck the camp of Demetrius: and took him, and brought him to Arsaces, and he put him into ward.
text.KJV: Who went and smote the hoste of Demetrius, and tooke him and brought him to Arsaces, by whom hee was put in warde.
As for the land of Iudea, that was quiet all the dayes of Simon: for he sought the good of his nation, in such wise, as that euermore his authoritie and honour pleased them well.
text.NABRE: 14:4 Praise of Simon The land was at rest all the days of Simon, who sought the good of his nation. His rule delighted his people and his glory all his days.
text.NRSV: The land had rest all the days of Simon. He sought the good of his nation; his rule was pleasing to them, as was the honor he enjoyed, all his days.
text.DRB: And all the land of Juda was at rest all the days of Simon, and he sought good to his nation: & his power, and his glory pleased them all days.
And with all his glory he took Joppe for an haven, and made it an entrance unto the isles of the sea.
text.KJV: And as he was honourable (in all his acts) so in this, that he tooke Ioppe for an hauen, and made an entrance to the yles of the Sea,

text.DRB: And he enlarged the borders of his nation, and obtained the country:
text.KJV: And enlarged the boundes of his nation, and recouered the countrey,

text.DRB: And gathered a great captivity, and had dominion of Gazara, and of Bethsura, and of the castle: and took away the uncleanness out of it, and there was none that resisted him.
text.KJV: And gathered together a great number of captiues, and had the dominion of Gazara and Bethsura, and the towre, out of the which he tooke all vncleannesse, neither was there any that resisted him.

text.DRB: And every man tilled his land with peace: and the land of Juda yielded her fruits, and the trees of the fields their fruit.
text.KJV: Then did they till their ground in peace, and the earth gaue her increase, and the trees of the field their fruit.

text.DRB: The ancients sat all in the streets, and treated of the good things of the land, and the youngmen did on them glory, and the stoles of war.
text.KJV: The ancient men sate all in the streetes, communing together of good things, and the young men put on glorious and warrelike apparell.

text.DRB: And he gave victuals to the cities, and he appointed them that they should be vessels of munition, till the name of his glory was renowned even to the end of the earth.
text.KJV: He prouided victuals for the cities, and set in them all maner of munition, so that his honourable name was renowmed vnto the end of the world.

text.DRB: He made peace upon the land, and Israel rejoiced with great joy.
text.KJV: He made peace in the land, and Israel reioyced with great ioy:

text.DRB: And every man sat under his vine, and under his figtree: and there was none to make them afraid.
text.KJV: For euery man sate vnder his vine, and his figgetree, and there was none to fray them:

text.DRB: There was none left that impugned them upon the earth: kings were discomfited in those days.
text.KJV: Neither was there any left in the lande to fight against them: yea, the Kings themselues were ouerthrowen in those dayes.

text.DRB: And he confirmed all the humble of his people, and sought the law, & took away every wicked and evil man:
text.KJV: Moreouer hee strengthened all those of his people that were brought low: the Law he searched out, and euery contemner of the Law, and wicked person, he tooke away.

text.DRB: he glorified the holies, and multiplied the vessels of the holy places.
text.KJV: He beautified the Sanctuary, and multiplied the vessels of the Temple.

text.DRB: And it was heard at Rome that Jonathas was dead, & even unto the Spartiates: and they were very sorry.
text.KJV: Now when it was heard at Rome, & as far as Sparta, that Ionathan was dead, they were very sorie.

text.DRB: But as they heard that Simon his brother was made the High-Priest in his place, and he obtained all the country, and the cities therein;
text.KJV: But assoone as they heard that his brother Simon was made high Priest in his stead, and ruled the countrey, and the cities therein,

text.DRB: they wrote to him in brazen tables, to renew the amities and society, which they had made with Judas, & with Jonathas his brethren.
text.KJV: They wrote vnto him in tables of brasse, to renew the friendship & league which they had made with Iudas and Ionathan his brethren:

text.DRB: And they were read in the sight of the Church in Jerusalem. And this is a copy of the epistles, that the Spartiates sent.
text.KJV: Which writings were read before the Congregation at Ierusalem.

text.DRB: The Princes and the cities of the Spartians to Simon the grand Priest, & to the ancients, and the Priests, and the rest of the people of the Jews their brethren, greeting.
text.KJV: And this is the copy of the letters that the Lacedemonians sent: The rulers of the Lacedemonians, with the city, vnto Simon the high Priest, and the Elders and Priestes, and residue of the people of the Iewes, our brethren, send greeting.

text.DRB: The legates, that were sent to our people, have told us of your glory, and honour, and joy: and we rejoiced at their entrance.
text.KJV: The Embassadors that were sent vnto our people, certified vs of your glory and honour, wherefore we were glad of their comming,

text.DRB: And we wrote that, which was said of them in the counsels of the people, thus: Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the son of Jason, legates of the Jews, came to us, renewing with us old amity.
text.KJV: And did register the things that they spake, in the counsell of the people, in this maner: Numenius sonne of Antiochus, and Antipater sonne of Iason, the Iewes Embassadours, came vnto vs, to renew the friendship they had with vs.

text.DRB: And it pleased the people to receive the men gloriously, and to put a copy of their words in the severed book of the people, to be for a memory to the people of the Spartiates. And a copy of these we have written to Simon the grand Priest.
text.KJV: And it pleased the people to entertaine the men honourably, and to put the copy of their embassage in publike records, to the end the people of the Lacedemonians might haue a memoriall therof: furthermore we haue written a copy thereof vnto Simon the hie Priest.

text.DRB: And after these things Simon sent Numenius to Rome, having a great buckler of gold, of the weight of thousand pounds, to establish amity with them. But when the people of Rome had heard
text.KJV: After this, Simon sent Numenius to Rome, with a great shield of golde of a thousand pound weight, to confirme the league with them.

text.DRB: these words, they said: What thanks-giving shall we render to Simon & his children?
text.KJV: Whereof when the people heard, they said, What thankes shall wee giue to Simon and his sonnes?

text.DRB: For he hath restored his brethren, and hath expugned the enemies of Israel from them: and they decreed him liberty, & registered it in tables of brass, & put it in titles in mount Sion.
text.KJV: For hee and his brethren, and the house of his father, haue established Israel, and chased away in fight their enemies from them, and confirmed their libertie.

So then they wrote in tables of brasse, which they set vpon pillars in mount Sion, and this is the copie of the writing. The eighteenth day of the moneth Elul, in the hundred threescore and twelft yeere, being the third yeere of Simon the hie priest,
text.NABRE: 14:27 The following is a copy of the inscription: “On the eighteenth day of Elul, in the one hundred and seventy-second year, that is, the third year under Simon the great high priest in Asaramel,
text.NRSV: This is a copy of what they wrote on bronze tablets, which they set up on pillars on Mount Zion:
text.DRB: And this is a copy of the writing. The Eighteenth day of the month Elul, in the year an hundreth seventy two, the third year under Simon the grand-Priest in Asaramel,
in a great assembly of the Priests, and of the people & the Princes of the nation, and the ancients of the country, these things were notified: that there have often been battles made in our country.
text.KJV: At Saramel in the great congregation of the priests and people, and rulers of the nation, & elders of the country, were these things notified vnto vs.

text.DRB: But Simon the son of Mathathias of the children of Jarib, & his brethren put themselves in danger, and resisted the adversaries of their nation, that their holies and law might stand: and with great glory have they glorified their nation.
text.KJV: Forsomuch as often times there haue bin warres in the countrey, wherin for the maintenance of their Sanctuarie, and the law, Simon the sonne of Mattathias of the posteritie of Iarib, together with his brethren, put themselues in ieopardie, and resisting the enemies of their nation, did their nation great honour.

text.DRB: And Jonathas gathered together his nation, & was made their grand Priest, & he was laid to his people.
text.KJV: (For after that Ionathan hauing gathered his nation together, and bene their hie priest, was added to his people,

text.DRB: And their enemies would conculcate, and destroy their country, and extend their hands against the holies.
text.KJV: Their enemies purposed to inuade their countrey that they might destroy it, and lay hands on the Sanctuary.

text.DRB: Then Simon, resisted and fought for his nation, and bestowed much money, and armed the valiant men of his nation, and gave them wages:
text.KJV: At which time Simon rose vp, and fought for his nation, and spent much of his own substance, & armed the valiant men of his nation, & gaue them wages,

text.DRB: and he fortified the cities of Jurie, and Bethsura, that was in the borders of Jurie, where the armour of the enemies was before: and he placed there Jews for a garrison.
text.KJV: And fortified the cities of Iudea, together with Bethsura that lieth vpon the borders of Iudea, where the armour of the enemies had bin before, but he set a garison of Iewes there.

text.DRB: And he fortified Joppe, which was by the sea: and Gazara, which is in the borders of Azotus, wherein the enemies dwelt before, and he placed Jews there: and whatsoever was fit for their correption, he put in them.
text.KJV: Moreouer, hee fortified Ioppe which lieth vpon the Sea, and Gazara that bordereth vpon Azotus, where the enemies had dwelt before: but hee placed Iewes there, and furnished them with all things conuenient for the reparation thereof.)

text.DRB: And the people saw the fact of Simon, and the glory that he meant to do to his nation, and they made him their duke, and grand Priest, for that he had done all these things, and for the justice, and faith, which he kept to his nation, and for that he sought by all means to advance his people.
text.KJV: The people therefore seeing the acts of Simon, and vnto what glory he thought to bring his nation, made him their gouernor and chiefe priest, because he had done all these things, and for the iustice and faith which hee kept to his nation, and for that hee sought by all meanes to exalt his people.

text.DRB: And in his days it prospered in his hands, so that the heathen were taken away out of their country, and they that were in the city of David in Jerusalem in the castle, out of which they came forth, and contaminated all things that were round about the holies, and they brought great plague upon chastity:
text.KJV: For in his time things prospered in his hands, so that the heathen were taken out of their countrey, and they also that were in the citie of Dauid in Ierusalem, who had made themselues a towre, out of which they issued, and polluted all about the Sanctuarie, and did much hurt in the holy place.

text.DRB: and he placed in it men of Jurie to the defence of the country, and of the city, and he raised the walls of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: But he placed Iewes therein, and fortified it for the safetie of the countrey, and the city, and raised vp the wals of Ierusalem.

text.DRB: And king Demetrius appointed him the high priesthood.
text.KJV: King Demetrius also confirmed him in the high priesthood, according to those things,

text.DRB: According to these things he made him his friend, and glorified him with great glory.
text.KJV: And made him one of his friends, and honoured him with great honour.

text.DRB: For he heard that the Jews were called of the Romans, friends, & fellows, & brethren, & that they received Simon's legates gloriously:
text.KJV: For he had heard say, that the Romanes had called the Iewes their friends, and confederates, and brethren, and that they had entertained the Embassadours of Simon honourably.
Also that the Iewes & priests were wel pleased that Simon should be their gouernour, and high priest for euer vntil there should arise a faithfull prophet.
text.NABRE: 14:41 and that the Jewish people and their priests had decided the following: Simon shall be their leader and high priest forever until a trustworthy prophet arises.
text.NRSV: “The Jews and their priests have resolved that Simon should be their leader and high priest forever, until a trustworthy prophet should arise.”
text.DRB: and that the Jews, and their Priests consented that he should be their duke, & high-Priest for ever, till there rise the faithful Prophet:
and that he be duke over them, and that he should have the care of the holies, and that he should appoint rulers over their works, and over the country, and over the armour, and over the holds.
text.KJV: Moreouer, that he should be their captaine, and should take charge of the Sanctuarie, to set them ouer their workes, and ouer the countrey, and ouer the armour, and ouer the fortresses, that (I say) he should take charge of the Sanctuarie.

text.DRB: And that he have care of the holies: and that he be heard of all, and that all writings in the country be written in his name: and that he be clothed with purple and gold:
text.KJV: Besides this, that he should be obeyed of euery man, and that all the writings in the countrey should be made in his name, and that he should be clothed in purple, and weare gold.

text.DRB: and that it be not lawful for any of the people, and of the Priests to disannul any of these, and to gainsay those things, that are said of him, or to call together an assembly in the country without him: & to be clothed with purple, & to wear a chain of gold.
text.KJV: Also that it should be lawfull for none of the people or priests, to breake any of these things, or to gainesay his words, or to gather an assembly in the countrey without him, or to bee clothed in purple, or weare a buckle of gold.

text.DRB: But he that shall do beside these things, or shall make frustrate any of these, he shall be guilty.
text.KJV: And whosoeuer should do otherwise, or breake any of these things, he should be punished.

text.DRB: And it pleased all the people to appoint Simon, and to do according to these words.
text.KJV: Thus it liked all þ people to deale with Simon, & to do as hath bene said.

text.DRB: And Simon took it upon him, and it pleased him to execute high Priest-hood, and to be duke & prince of the nation of the Jews, and of the Priests, and to be chief over all.
text.KJV: Then Simon accepted hereof, and was well pleased to be high Priest, and captaine, and gouernour of the Iewes, & priests, & to defend them all.

text.DRB: And this writing they bade them put in tables of brass, and to set them in the circuit of the holies, in a famous place:
text.KJV: So they commanded that this writing should be put in tables of brasse, and that they should be set vp within the compasse of the Sanctuary in a conspicuous place.

text.DRB: and to put a copy thereof in the treasury, that Simon may have it, and his children.
text.KJV: Also þ the copies therof should be laid vp in the treasurie, to the ende that Simon & his sonnes might haue them.
Moreouer Antiochus sonne of Demetrius the king, sent letters from the isles of the Sea, vnto Simon the priest, and prince of the Iewes, and to all the people.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 Letter of Antiochus VII. Antiochus, son of King Demetrius, sent a letter from the islands of the sea to Simon, the priest and ethnarch of the Jews, and to all the nation,
text.NRSV: Antiochus, son of King Demetrius, sent a letter from the islands of the sea to Simon, the priest and ethnarch of the Jews, and to all the nation.
text.DRB: And king Antiochus the son of Demetrius sent epistles from the isles of the sea to Simon the priest, and prince of the nation of the Jews, and to all the nation:
and they contained this tenure: King Antiochus to Simon the grand-Priest, and to the nation of the Jews greeting.
text.KJV: The contents whereof were these: King Antiochus, to Simon the high Priest, and prince of his nation, and to the people of the Iewes, greeting,

text.DRB: Because certain pestilent men have obtained the kingdom of our fathers, and I mean to challenge the kingdom, and to restore it as it was before: and I have chosen a great army, and have made ships of war.
text.KJV: For as much as certaine pestilent men, haue vsurped the kingdome of our fathers, and my purpose is to chalenge it againe, that I may restore it to the old estate, and to that end haue gathered a multitude of forraine souldiers together, and prepared shippes of warre,

text.DRB: And I will march through the country, that I may take revenge of them, that have destroyed our country, and that have made many cities desolate in my realm.
text.KJV: My meaning also being to goe through the countrey, that I may be auenged of them that haue destroyed it, and made many cities in the kingdome desolate:

text.DRB: Now therefore I establish unto thee all the oblations, which all the kings before me remitted unto thee, & what other gifts soever they remitted thee:
text.KJV: Now therefore I confirme vnto thee, all the oblations which the kings before me granted thee, and whatsoeuer gifts besides they granted.

text.DRB: and I permit thee to make a coin of thy own money in thy country:
text.KJV: I giue thee leaue also to coine money for thy countrey with thine owne stampe.

text.DRB: and Jerusalem to be holy and free, and all the armour that is made, and the fortresses which thou hast built, and which thou dost hold, let them remain to thee.
text.KJV: And as concerning Ierusalem, and the Sanctuarie, let them be free, and al the armour that thou hast made, and fortresses that thou hast built, and keepest in thy hands, let them remaine vnto thee.

text.DRB: And all that is due to the king, and the things that are to be the king's hereafter, from this present and for all time, they are remitted to thee.
text.KJV: And if any thing bee, or shall be owing to the king, let it be forgiuen thee, from this time forth for euermore.

text.DRB: And when we shall have obtained our kingdom, we will glorify thee, and thy nation, & the temple with great glory, so that your glory shall be made manifest in all the earth.
text.KJV: Furthermore, when we haue obtained our kingdome, we will honour thee, and thy nation, and thy temple with great honour, so that your honour shall bee knowen throughout the world.
In the hundred threescore and fourteenth yeere, went Antiochus into the land of his fathers, at which time all the forces came together vnto him, so that few were left with Tryphon.
text.NABRE: 15:10 In the one hundred and seventy-fourth year Antiochus invaded the land of his ancestors, and all the troops rallied to him, so that few were left with Trypho.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred seventy-fourth year Antiochus invaded the land of his ancestors.
text.DRB: In the year an hundreth seventy four went forth Antiochus into the land of his fathers, and all the hosts assembled unto him, so that there were few left with Tryphon.
And Antiochus the king pursued him, and he came to Dora flying by the sea coast.
text.KJV: Wherefore being pursued by king Antiochus, he fled vnto Dora, which lieth by the Seaside.

text.DRB: For he knew that evils were heaped upon him, and the army forsook him.
text.KJV: For he saw, that troubles came vpon him all at once, and that his forces had forsaken him.

text.DRB: And Antiochus camped upon Dora with an hundred twenty thousand men of war, & eight thousand horsemen:
text.KJV: Then camped Antiochus against Dora, hauing with him, an hundred and twentie thousand men of warre, and eight thousand horsemen.

text.DRB: and he compassed the city, and the ships approached to the sea: & they vexed the city by land & by sea, and suffered none to come in, or to go out.
text.KJV: And when he had compassed the citie round about, and ioyned ships close to the towne on the Sea side, hee vexed the citie by land, and by Sea, neither suffered he any to goe out or in.

text.DRB: And Numenius came, and they that had been with him, from the city of Rome, having epistles written to kings, and countries, wherein were contained these words:
text.KJV: In the meane season came Numenius, & his company from Rome hauing letters to the kings and countries, wherein were written these things.

text.DRB: Lucius the Consul of the Romans, to Ptolemee the king greeting.
text.KJV: Lucius, Consul of the Romanes, vnto king Ptolomee greeting.

text.DRB: The Ambassadors of the Jews our friends came to us, renewing the old amity, and society, being sent from Simon the prince of the priests, and the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: The Iewes Embassadors our friends and confederates, came vnto vs to renew the old friendship and league, being sent from Simon the high Priest, and from the people of the Iewes.

text.DRB: And they brought also a buckler of gold of a thousand pounds.
text.KJV: And they brought a shield of gold, of a thousand pound:

text.DRB: It hath pleased us therefore to write to the kings, & countries, that they do them no harm, nor impugn them, & their cities, and their countries: and that they give no aid to them that fight against them.
text.KJV: We thought it good therefore, to write vnto the kings and countries, that they should doe them no harme, nor fight against them, their cities, or countries, nor yet aide their enemies against them.

text.DRB: And it hath seemed good unto us to take the buckler of them.
text.KJV: It seemed also good to vs, to receiue the shield of them.

text.DRB: If therefore any pestilent men are fled out of their country to you, deliver them to Simon the Prince of the Priests, that he may punish them according to their law.
text.KJV: If therefore there be any pestilent fellowes, that haue fled from their countrie vnto you, deliuer them vnto Simon the high priest, that hee may punish them according to their owne lawe.

text.DRB: These self same things were written to Demetrius the king, and to Attalus, and to Ariarathes, and to Arsaces,
text.KJV: The same thing wrote hee likewise vnto Demetrius the king, and Attalus, to Ariarathes, and Arsaces,

text.DRB: and into all countries: and to Lampsaces, & to the Spartiates, and to Delus, and to Myndus, and to Sicyon, & to Caria, and to Samus, & Pamphilia, and Lycia, and Alicarnassus, and Coo, & Siden, & Aradon, and Rhodes, and Phaselis, & Gortyna, and Gnidus, and Cypres, & Cyrenee.
text.KJV: And to all the countries, and to Sampsames, & the Lacedemonians, and to Delus, and Myndus, and Sycion, and Caria, and Samos, and Pamphylia, and Lycia, and Halicarnassus, and Rhodus, and Phaseilis, and Cos, and Sidee, and Aradus, and Gortina, and Cnidus, and Cyprus, and Cyrene.

text.DRB: And a copy thereof they wrote to Simon the prince of the priests and people of the Jews.
text.KJV: And the copy heereof they wrote, to Simon the high Priest.

text.DRB: But king Antiochus moved his camp unto Dora the second time, setting hands always upon it, and making engines: & he shut up Tryphon, that he could not go forth.
text.KJV: So Antiochus the king camped against Dora, the second day, assaulting it continually, and making engins, by which meanes he shut vp Tryphon, that he could neither goe out nor in.

text.DRB: And Simon sent unto him two thousand chosen men for aid, and silver, & gold, and aboundance of furniture.
text.KJV: At that time Simon sent him two thousand chosen men to aide him: siluer also, and gold, and much armour.

text.DRB: And he would not take them, but brake all things that he covenanted with him before, and alienated himself from him.
text.KJV: Neuerthelesse, he would not receiue them, but brake all the couenants which he had made with him afore, and became strange vnto him.

text.DRB: And he sent to him Athenobius one of his friends, to treat with him, saying: You hold Joppe, and Gazara, and the castle, that is in Jerusalem, cities of my kingdom:
text.KJV: Furthermore hee sent vnto him Athenobius, one of his friends to commune with him and say: you withhold Ioppe and Gazara with the towre that is in Ierusalem, which are cities of my realme.

text.DRB: their borders you have made desolate, and you have made a great plague in the land, and have ruled in many places in my kingdom.
text.KJV: The borders thereof yee haue wasted and done great hurt in the land, and got the dominion of many places within my kingdome.

text.DRB: Now therefore deliver the cities, that you have taken, and the tributes of the places, wherein you have ruled without the borders of Jury.
text.KJV: Now therefore deliuer the cities which ye haue taken, and the tributes of the places whereof yee haue gotten dominion without the borders of Iudea.

text.DRB: But if not, give you for them five hundred talents of silver, and for the destruction, that you have made, & the tributes of cities other five hundred talents: but if not, we will come and expugn you.
text.KJV: Or else giue me for them fiue hundred talents of siluer, and for the harme that you haue done, and the tributes of the cities other fiue hundred talents: if not, we wil come and fight against you.

text.DRB: And Athenobius the king's friend came into Jerusalem, and saw the glory of Simon, and his magnificence in gold, and silver, and furniture aboundant, and he was astonished, & told him the king's words.
text.KJV: So Athenobius the kings friend came to Ierusalem, and when hee saw the glory of Simon, and the cupboard of gold, and siluer plate, and his great attendance, he was astonished and told him the kings message.

text.DRB: And Simon answered him, and said to him: Neither have we taken other men's land, neither do we hold that is other men's: but the inheritance of our fathers, which was a certain season unjustly possessed of our enemies.
text.KJV: Then answered Simon, and said vnto him, We haue neither taken other mens land, nor holden that which apperteineth to others, but the inheritance of our fathers, which our enemies had wrongfully in possession a certaine time.

text.DRB: But we having opportunity challenge the inheritance of our fathers.
text.KJV: Wherefore we hauing opportunitie, hold the inheritance of our fathers.

text.DRB: For concerning Joppe, and Gazara, that thou complainest they made a great plague among the people, and in our country: for these we given an hundred talents. And Athenobius answered him not a word.
text.KJV: And whereas thou demaundest Ioppe and Gazara; albeit they did great harme vnto the people in our countrey, yet will we giue an hundred talents for them. Hereunto Athenobius answered him not a word,

text.DRB: But with anger returning to the king, he reported unto him these words, and the glory of Simon, and all things that he saw, and the king was wrath with great anger.
text.KJV: But returned in a rage to the king, and made report vnto him of these speaches, and of the glory of Simon, and of all that hee had seene: whereupon the king was exceeding wroth.

text.DRB: And Tryphon fled by ship to Orthosias
text.KJV: In the meane time fled Tryphon by ship vnto Orthosias.

text.DRB: And the king appointed Cendebæus captain by the sea coast, and gave him an army of foot men and horsemen.
text.KJV: Then the king made Cendebeus captaine of the sea coast, and gaue him an hoste of footmen and horsemen,

text.DRB: And he commanded him to move the camp against the face of Jurie: and he commanded him to build Cedron, and to fortify the gates of the city, and to conquer the people. But the king pursued Tryphon.
text.KJV: And commanded him to remoue his hoste toward Iudea: also hee commanded him to build vp Cedron, and to fortifie the gates, & to warre against the people, but as for the king he pursued Tryphon.

text.DRB: And Cendebæus came to Jamnia, and began to provoke the people, and to conculcate Jurie, & to take the people captive, and to kill, and to build Cedron.
text.KJV: So Cendebeus came to Iamnia, and began to prouoke the people, and to inuade Iudea, and to take the people prisoners, and slay them.

text.DRB: And he placed there horsemen, and an host: that going forth they might walk by the way of Jurie, as the king appointed him.
text.KJV: And when hee had built vp Cedron, he set horsemen there, and an host to the end that issuing out, they might make outroades vpon the wayes of Iudea, as the king had commanded him.
Then came vp Iohn from Gazara, and told Simon his father, what Cendebeus had done.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 John then went up from Gazara and told his father Simon what Cendebeus was doing.
text.NRSV: John went up from Gazara and reported to Simon his father what Trypho was doing.
text.DRB: And John went up from Gazara, and told Simon his father what Cendebæus did among their people.

And Simon called his two elder sons, Judas and John, and he said to them: I and my brethren, and the house of my father, have expugned the enemies of Israel from our youth even to this day: and it hath prospered in our hands to deliver Israel oftentimes.
text.KJV: Wherefore Simon called his two eldest sonnes, Iudas and Iohn, and said vnto them, I and my brethren, and my fathers house haue euer from our youth vnto this day fought against the enemies of Israel, and things haue prospered so well in our hands, that wee haue deliuered Israel oftentimes.

text.DRB: And now I am old, but be you in place of me, and my brethren, and going forth fight for our nation: and the help from heaven be with you.
text.KJV: But now I am old, and yee are of a sufficient age: Be ye in stead of mee, and my brother, and goe and fight for our nation, and the helpe from heauen be with you.

text.DRB: And he chose out of the country twenty thousand fighting men and horsemen, and they went forth to Cendebæus: and they slept in Modin.
text.KJV: So hee chose out of the countrey twentie thousand men of warre with horsemen, who went out against Cendebeus, and rested that night at Modin.

text.DRB: And they arose in the morning, and went into the plain field: and lo a mighty great army coming to meet them of foot men, and horsemen, & there was a running river between them.
text.KJV: And when as they rose in the morning, and went into the plaine, behold, a mighty great hoste both of footmen, and horsmen, came against them: Howbeit there was a water brooke betwixt them.

text.DRB: And he camped against the face of them, himself and his people, and he saw the people fearful to pass through the torrent, and he passed through first: and the men saw him, and they passed over after him.
text.KJV: So hee and his people pitched ouer against them, and when hee saw that the people were afraid to goe ouer the water brooke, hee went first ouer himselfe, and then the men seeing him, passed through after him.

text.DRB: And he divided the people, and the horsemen in the midst of the footmen: and the horsemen of the adversaries were exceeding many.
text.KJV: he diuided his men, and set the horsemen in the midst of the footemen: for the enemies horsemen were very many.

text.DRB: And they sounded with the holy trumpets: and Cendebæus was put to flight, and his camp: and there fell many of them wounded, & the rest fled into the hold.
text.KJV: Then sounded they with the holy Trumpets: whereupon Cendebeus and his hoste were put to flight, so that many of them were slaine, and the remnant gat them to the strong hold.

text.DRB: Then was Judas John's brother wounded: But John pursued them, till he came to Cedron, which he built:
text.KJV: At that time was Iudas Iohns brother wounded: But Iohn still followed after them, vntill he came to Cedron which had built.

text.DRB: and they fled even to the towers, that were in the fields of Azotus, and he burnt them with fire. And there fell of them two thousand men, and he returned into Jurie in peace.
text.KJV: So they fled euen vnto the towres in the fields of Azotus, wherefore hee burnt it with fire: So that there were slaine of them about two thousand men. Afterward hee returned into the land of Iudea in peace.

text.DRB: And Ptolomee the son of Abobus was appointed captain in the plain of Jericho, and he had much silver, & gold.
text.KJV: Moreouer, in the plaine of Iericho was Ptolomeus the sonne of Abubus made captaine, and hee had abundance of siluer and golde.

text.DRB: For he was the son in law of the high-Priest.
text.KJV: For he was the hie Priests sonne in lawe.

text.DRB: And his heart was exalted, and he would gain the country, and he purposed treachery against Simon and his sons to dispatch them.
text.KJV: Wherefore his heart being lifted vp, hee thought to get the countrey to himselfe, and thereupon consulted deceitfully against Simon and his sons, to destroy them.

Now Simon was visiting the cities that were in the countrey, and taking care for the good ordering of them, at which time hee came downe himselfe to Iericho with his sons, Mattathias and Iudas, in the hundreth threescore and seuenth yeere, in the eleuenth moneth called Sabat.
text.NABRE: 16:14 As Simon was inspecting the cities of the country and providing for their needs, he and his sons Mattathias and Judas went down to Jericho in the one hundred and seventy-seventh year, in the eleventh month (that is, the month Shebat).
text.NRSV: Simon was visiting the towns of the country and providing for their care when he went down to Jericho with his sons Mattathias and Judas.
text.DRB: And Simon walking through the cities, that were in the country of Jurie, and being careful of them, went down into Jericho, he and Mathathias his son, and Judas, the year an hundreth seventy seven, the eleventh month: this is the month Sabath.
And the son of Abobus received them with guile into a little fortress, that is called Doch, which he built, and he made them a great feast, and hid men there.
text.KJV: Where the sonne of Abubus receiuing them deceitfully into a little holde called Docus, which he had built, made them a great banquet: howbeit he had hidde men there.

So when Simon and his sonnes had drunke largely, Ptolome and his men rose vp, and tooke their weapons, and came vpon Simon into the banketting place, and slewe him and his two sonnes, and certaine of his seruants.
text.NABRE: 16:16 Then, when Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men sprang up, weapons in hand, rushed upon Simon in the banquet hall, and killed him, his two sons, and some of his servants.
text.NRSV: When Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men rose up, took their weapons, rushed in against Simon in the banquet hall and killed him and his two sons and some of his servants.
text.DRB: And when Simon was inebriated & his sons, Ptolomee arose with his company, and they took their weapons, and entered into the feast, and slew him, and his two sons, and certain of his servants.
And he did great deceit in Israel, and rendered evil for good.
text.KJV: In which doing, he committed a great treachery, and recompensed euill for good.

text.DRB: And Ptolomee wrote these things, and sent to the king that he should send him an army for aid, and he would deliver him the country, and their cities, and tributes.
text.KJV: Then Ptolome wrote these things, and sent to the king, that he should send him an hoste to aide him, and he would deliuer him the countrey and cities.

text.DRB: And he sent others into Gazara to dispatch John: and to the tribunes he sent epistles, that they should come to him, and he would give them silver, and gold, and gifts.
text.KJV: He sent others also to Gazara to kill Iohn, & vnto the tribunes he sent letters to come vnto him, that he might giue them siluer, and golde, & rewards.

text.DRB: And he sent others to take Jerusalem, and the mount of the temple.
text.KJV: And others he sent to take Ierusalem, and the mountaine of the temple.

text.DRB: And one running before told John in Gazara, that his father was perished, and his brethren, and that he hath sent that thou also mayst be slain.
text.KJV: Now one had runne afore to Gazara, and tolde Iohn that his father and brethren were slaine, and Ptolome hath sent to slay thee also.

text.DRB: But as he heard it, he was exceedingly afraid: and he apprehended the men, that came to destroy him, and he slew them: for he knew that they sought to destroy him.
text.KJV: Hereof when he heard, hee was sore astonished: So he laide hands on them that were come to destroy him, and slew them, for hee knew that they sought to make him away.
As concerning the rest of the actes of Iohn, and his wars & worthy deeds which hee did, and the building of the walles which he made, and his doings,
text.NABRE: 16:23 Now the rest of the acts of John, his wars and the brave deeds he performed, his rebuilding of the walls, and all his achievements—
text.NRSV: The rest of the acts of John and his wars and the brave deeds that he did... are written in the annals of his high priesthood.
text.DRB: And the rest concerning the words of John, and his battles, and the valiant acts, which he did manfully, and the building of the walls, which he built, and the things that he did:
lo these are written in the book of the days of his priesthood, from the time that he was made prince of the priests after his father.
text.KJV: Behold, these are written in the Chronicles of his Priesthood, from the time he was made high Priest after his father.
